directScanItemizedRecords.title: C. Boyden Gray memorandum to the President re invitation to Supreme Court Justices to lunch at Camp David
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Boyden Gray discusses inviting Supreme Court justices and additional guests to lunch at Camp David, with presidential handwriting and an attached guest list.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Response of the Administration to Issues Raised in Connection with the Negotiation of a North American Free Trade Agreement
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President transmits the administration's response to congressional issues raised over fast-track authority for a North American free trade agreement with Mexico.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Tab 1: Free Trade Negotiation with Mexico, Economic Impact
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The economic-impact tab assesses trade with Mexico, sector and worker adjustment, U.S. competitiveness, environmental trade issues, and likely effects of a North American free trade agreement.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Tab 4: Free Trade Negotiations with Mexico, Environmental Matters
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The environmental-matters tab reviews Mexico's environmental law, U.S.-Mexico environmental cooperation, and environmental safeguards connected to NAFTA negotiations.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Memorandum of Understanding re U.S.-Mexico labor cooperation
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A U.S.-Mexico labor cooperation memorandum of understanding sets out cooperation between the U.S. Department of Labor and Mexico's Secretariat of Labor and Social Welfare.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Tab 3: Free Trade Negotiations with Mexico, Labor Standards, Worker Health and Safety, and Worker Rights
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The labor tab examines labor standards, worker health and safety, and worker rights issues raised by fast-track consideration of free trade negotiations with Mexico.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Tab 2: Free Trade Negotiations with Mexico, Facilitating Adjustment
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The adjustment tab reviews how the administration proposed to facilitate worker and firm adjustment during negotiations toward a North American free trade agreement.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Pool reports on National Security Agency visit, Great American Workout, Lee Sang-Ock photo op, and fast-track briefing
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The May 1 pool-report packet covers the President's National Security Agency visit, Great American Workout, Lee Sang-Ock photo opportunity, and fast-track briefing.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Office of the Press Secretary release of remarks to fast-track coalition supporters
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Office of the Press Secretary released the President's remarks to fast-track coalition supporters in Room 450 of the Old Executive Office Building.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Office of the Press Secretary release of remarks to farm broadcasters
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Office of the Press Secretary released the President's April 29 remarks to farm broadcasters in the Roosevelt Room.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Fact Sheet: The Administration's Action Plan in response to NAFTA negotiation issues
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The administration fact sheet summarizes its action plan responding to concerns raised in connection with negotiating a North American free trade agreement.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary: 5:00 p.m. news update, April 30, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The April 30 White House News Summary update tracks fast-track, Mexico trade, and related press coverage before the May 1 transmittal.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary updates, May 1, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The May 1 White House News Summary pages include 9:30 a.m., 12:45 p.m., and 4:30 p.m. updates on fast-track and the day's press coverage.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Legislative Issues Update cover sheet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Cover sheet for the Office of Legislative Affairs Legislative Issues Update dated May 10, 1990.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Frederick D. McClure memorandum to President Bush re Legislative Issues Update
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Frederick D. McClure transmits an updated legislative issues analysis to the President, noting that the Office of Legislative Affairs will continue to update the summary as issues develop.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Office of Legislative Affairs Legislative Issues Update, May 10, 1990
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Office of Legislative Affairs update surveys major issues before Congress, including abortion, ADA, Amtrak, appropriations, budget, campaign finance, capital gains, child care, China/MFN sanctions, civil rights, crime and drugs, Eastern Europe, education, environment, export controls, farm policy, flag burning, food safety, supplemental appropriations, Hatch Act, housing, labor, national service, OIRA, pocket veto, product liability, RTC, Soviet trade, and motor voter legislation.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Burton J. Lee memorandum to John H. Sununu re AIDS
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Burton J. Lee advises John Sununu that President Bush's central AIDS theme should be education and prevention, with issues including promiscuity, drugs, and fairness to fellow Americans.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Newspaper clipping page with Sali Berisha essay excerpt and William Safire column In Deep Sununu
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A clipping page combines an essay excerpt on Albania's post-Communist politics with William Safire's column In Deep Sununu on John Sununu's White House role and travel controversies.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush correspondence with Johnny Morris re Big Cedar Lodge
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush writes Johnny Morris about the Big Cedar Lodge book and hopes to visit the shop again; Morris's June 20 incoming letter thanks Bush for interest in the Bluefin Tuna issue and invites him to Big Cedar Lodge.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush letter to George De B. Bell re birthday card and summer plans
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush writes George De B. Bell about a card, returning to golf and tennis, and plans to celebrate his mother's ninetieth birthday in Maine.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush note to Clark S. Judge with The Tax That Ate the Economy article
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A White House routing page carries President Bush's note thanking Clark S. Judge for his conclusion, followed by Judge's article The Tax That Ate the Economy on capital-gains taxation and job creation.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Proposed substitute for S. 323, Chafee Title X Pregnancy Counseling Act of 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The proposed substitute outlines pregnancy counseling options, family involvement, conscience protections, and availability of services under Title X funded clinics.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Senator Chafee memorandum to President Bush re Title X and Pregnancy Counseling
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Senator John Chafee asks President Bush to support Title X pregnancy counseling legislation that would overturn the 1988 HHS regulations and respond to medical, public-opinion, and Republican electoral concerns.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Vote lists on the Chafee pregnancy counseling amendment and S. 323
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Vote lists record Senate votes on the Chafee pregnancy counseling amendment to Title X and a Senate Labor and Human Resources Committee vote on S. 323.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: S. 323 Title X Pregnancy Counseling Act bill print and status sheet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes the S. 323 bill print title page and a status sheet for the Title X Pregnancy Counseling Act of 1991.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Wall Street Journal article: AMA Opposes Government Interference With Doctors' Counseling of Patients
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Thomas M. Burton reports that the American Medical Association opposed government interference with doctors' counseling of patients.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Washington Post editorial: Forbidden Advice
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Washington Post editorial Forbidden Advice criticizes restrictions on abortion-related advice in federally funded family-planning clinics.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: New York Times article: A.M.A. Condemns U.S. Curbs on Medical Advice
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The New York Times reports from Chicago that the American Medical Association condemned federal curbs on medical advice related to abortion counseling.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: American Medical Association statement: The Gag Rule
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The American Medical Association statement attributed to James S. Todd supports S. 323 and H.R. 392, the Title X Pregnancy Counseling Act.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists statement on pregnancy counseling
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Richard H. Schwarz, president of the American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists, argues that restrictions on pregnancy counseling interfere with the doctor-patient relationship.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: David Cole op-ed: Get Government Out of the Doctor's Office
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: David Cole's Washington Post op-ed criticizes government restrictions on what doctors may say to patients in Title X clinics.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Draft amendment on Title X pregnancy information and counseling
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Draft amendment language would require pregnancy information and counseling under Title X while addressing conscience protections, referrals, definitions, and religious or moral convictions.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush handwritten letter to Louis Marks Jr. re fan-jet forehand
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush writes Louis Marks Jr. in longhand about having been there at sixty years old and suggests that a fan-jet forehand might enhance his finesse.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush letter to Charles Neblett re Lisbon note and family news
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush thanks Charles Neblett for a June 9 note from Lisbon, mentions Joe and Carole Zappala, Andrew and Margaret, and plans for his mother's ninetieth birthday.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Pool report on bust unveiling and Thurgood Marshall resignation
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Jim Gerstenzang's pool report covers the bust unveiling and President Bush's comment on Justice Thurgood Marshall's resignation.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Office of the Press Secretary statement by the President on Justice Thurgood Marshall
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush praises Justice Thurgood Marshall's distinguished service as a civil-rights lawyer, judge, Solicitor General, and Supreme Court justice, and says he intends to nominate a successor very soon.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Pool reports on June 26 meetings with House Republicans and Senators
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Charlie Green's pool reports cover President Bush's June 26 meetings with House Republicans and senators, including questions on Yugoslavia, abortion counseling, MFN, and China.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary updates, June 27, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: White House News Summary updates for June 27 cover Yugoslavia, recession comments, Supreme Court decisions, Justice Marshall's retirement, the Middle East peace process, and related wire coverage.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: George Bush Presidential Library transfer sheet and photocopies of transferred White House photographs
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A Bush Library transfer sheet records White House photographs transferred to the audiovisual collection, followed by photocopies of the images in the daily-file scan.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: U.S. Savings Bonds: The Great American Investment, 1991 Campaign booklet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The 1991 U.S. Savings Bonds campaign booklet includes messages from President Bush, Secretary Nicholas Brady, and national chairman John Clendenin; campaign goals; volunteer committee rosters; honor rolls; and savings bond explanations.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: London Economic Summit dinner program and invitation for H.M. Tower of London
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The London Economic Summit dinner packet includes the Prime Minister's Tower of London invitation for George Bush, dinner program pages, notes on H.M. Tower of London, and menu and wine-list pages.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential movements in London, July 15, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A Presidential Movements page records President Bush's London, England movements for July 15, 1991.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House telephone memoranda for President Bush and Signal Switchboard, July 15, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Two telephone memorandum pages for President Bush and the Signal Switchboard record no calls for July 15, 1991.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary: 6:00 a.m. EDT edition, July 15, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The 6:00 a.m. White House News Summary covers the London economic summit, Gorbachev's appeal for aid, Iraq, Clarence Thomas, Cuomo, network news, and Sunday political programs.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Congressional Monitor: This Week in Congress, July 15, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Congressional Monitor, Vol. 27 No. 144, surveys the week in Congress, including House and Senate schedules, committee action, floor business, and legislative coverage.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Buckingham Palace dinner invitation for President and Mrs. Bush
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Buckingham Palace invitation packet invites President and Mrs. Bush, by command of Queen Elizabeth II and the Duke of Edinburgh, to dinner on July 16, 1991.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Acland event planning packet with Mrs. Bush note and President Bush handwriting
directScanItemizedRecords.type: Correspondence and Note Packet with President Bush Handwriting
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Acland event planning packet includes Mrs. Bush's request, notes to Patty, and President Bush handwriting about continuing an event for the Aclands before their July departure.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Marlin Fitzwater statement on Palestinian federation and Syrian acceptance at London
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Marlin Fitzwater issues a London statement on Palestinian federation and Syrian acceptance in connection with Middle East peace diplomacy.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Marlin Fitzwater press briefing at London Hilton Hotel, July 15, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Marlin Fitzwater press briefing transcript covers London summit questions, Middle East peace diplomacy, Soviet issues, START, and related foreign-policy questions.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Pool reports on Bush bilaterals and press logistics at Winfield House
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Pool reports cover President Bush's July 15 bilaterals at Winfield House, including meetings with Mitterrand, Mulroney, Kohl, and Andreotti, plus press logistics and summit observations.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: AP Wire Story by Edith M. Lederer: British bar reporters from covering leaders and spouses
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Edith M. Lederer's Associated Press wire story reports that British officials barred reporters from covering leaders and spouses during the London economic summit.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary: 5:30 p.m. London / 12:30 p.m. EDT edition, July 15, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The 5:30 p.m. London White House News Summary tracks summit coverage, Middle East diplomacy, domestic politics, and morning network news coverage.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary: 2:30 p.m. EDT / 7:30 p.m. London update, July 15, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The 2:30 p.m. EDT / 7:30 p.m. London update summarizes census decision coverage, summit developments, Brady, and other late-day news items.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush letter to Hector Salazar re Kennebunkport shirt
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush thanks Hector Salazar for a shirt, says he expects to use it in Kennebunkport, and notes that Marvin will get the shirt Salazar sent him.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush handwritten note to Mark Yudof re New York Times picture and University of Texas law school friends
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush writes Mark Yudof about a New York Times picture, Charles Alan Wright, University of Texas law school friends, and sends thanks and regards to Edith.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush handwritten note to Thomas W. Moseley re photographs, Camp David call, and Maine
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush thanks Thomas W. Moseley for photographs, mentions a Camp David call with Lud Ashley, says he is back in Maine, and asks about Moseley's fall schedule.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush handwritten note to Craig Shergold re card collection
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush writes Craig Shergold that Lud Ashley told him about Shergold's card collection and sends one of his cards to add to it.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush letter to Ted Stevens re salmon and recovery
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush thanks Senator Ted Stevens for salmon sent to Kennebunkport and hopes Stevens's recovery is speedy.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush letter to Jack O. Guy re Southeast Georgia Veterans Day Celebration
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush tells Jack O. Guy that he does not know whether he can attend the Southeast Georgia Veterans Day Celebration but will have schedulers give it close attention.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush letter to Prescott S. Bush Jr. transmitting Armando Ferla and William Edgar reply copies
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush sends Prescott S. Bush Jr. copies of letters to Armando Ferla and William Edgar and comments on vacation at Kennebunkport despite events in the Soviet Union.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush handwritten note to Charles R. Black Jr. re campaign organization counsel
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush thanks Charles Black for helpful input, says he has not finalized campaign organization decisions, and values Black's counsel.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush letter to Philip H. Grondin re Sturdivant Island Bluefish/Tuna Tournament
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush tells Philip Grondin that he missed the Sturdivant Island Bluefish/Tuna Tournament, appreciates the invitation, and calls it one of the best summers for blue fishing he can recall.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush letter to Mr. and Mrs. William Kockos re 49th wedding anniversary
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush congratulates Mr. and Mrs. William Kockos on their 49th wedding anniversary, with a handwritten staff note about sending the tie bar and stick pin.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush letter to Armando Ferla re missing May letter
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush writes Armando Ferla that Pres passed along Ferla's message about a May letter, says no record of the letter was found, and invites him to stay in touch.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush letter to William Edgar re Westminster Theological Seminary keynote invitation
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush thanks William Edgar for inviting him to keynote the Westminster Theological Seminary luncheon and says a scheduler will be in touch.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush letter to Herbie re surgery and recovery
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush tells Herbie that he and Barbara heard about the surgery, are thinking of him, and hope his recovery is speedy.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary: 3:30 p.m. EDT update, August 21, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The 3:30 p.m. update tracks Gorbachev's television statement, Yazov, Soviet coup developments, Yeltsin, and related wire coverage.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary: 1:00 p.m. EDT update, August 21, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The 1:00 p.m. update covers reports about Dmitri Yazov, Soviet army movement, the coup committee, Yeltsin, Gorbachev, and Bush administration reaction.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary: 10:00 a.m. news update, August 21, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The 10:00 a.m. news update covers the collapse of the Soviet coup, Yeltsin, Gorbachev, Lithuania, and U.S. administration reaction.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary: 8:30 a.m. news update, August 21, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The 8:30 a.m. news update covers the Gang of Eight, Yeltsin, Soviet television, Bush statements, the Baltics, and early coup-collapse reporting.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Romania Jackson-Vanik waiver release packet, letter, executive order, determination, and duplicate copies
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Romania waiver packet includes press secretary statements, the President's transmittal letter to Congress, an executive order, and Presidential Determination No. 91-48 waiving Trade Act emigration provisions for Romania.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Remarks by President Bush during Walker's Point press availability on Gorbachev, duplicate transcript copies
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Two transcript copies record President Bush's Walker's Point press availability on the Soviet coup, Gorbachev's status, popular elections, the five plotters, and the coup's collapse.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Pool Report #24: post-press conference, August 21, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Pool Report #24 covers the motorcade back to Walker's Point, Fitzwater comments after the press conference, the Gorbachev call, and the President's informal shirt appearance.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press conference by President Bush at the Shawmut Inn, August 21, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Shawmut Inn press conference transcript covers Bush's response to the Soviet coup, Gorbachev, Yeltsin, Baker's European consultations, the Baltics, democracy, and U.S. policy toward the Soviet Union.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press Secretary statement on FY 1992 budget amendment for Chemical Safety and Hazard Investigation Board
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The statement announces that President Bush transmitted an FY 1992 budget amendment to establish the Chemical Safety and Hazard Investigation Board authorized by the Clean Air Act Amendments of 1990.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Washington Times Inside the Beltway clipping re John Banzhaf and lawyer jokes
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A Washington Times Inside the Beltway clipping includes item text on John Banzhaf and reactions to a lawyer-joke item.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: George Bush Presidential Library transfer sheet and photocopies of transferred White House photographs, August 21, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A Bush Library transfer sheet records White House photographs transferred to the audiovisual collection, followed by photocopies of official White House photographs from August 21, 1991.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary: network coverage following the President's address, duplicate copies
directScanItemizedRecords.type: White House News Summary Duplicate Copies
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Duplicate copies of a White House News Summary page excerpt ABC, CNN, and NBC commentary after President Bush's address on nuclear arms and defense policy.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary: 6:30 p.m. network evening news coverage of upcoming defense policy address, duplicate copies
directScanItemizedRecords.type: White House News Summary Duplicate Copies
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Duplicate five-page copies summarize ABC, NBC, and CBS evening news coverage before the President's address on defense policy and nuclear-arms reductions.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: AP Wire Story: Democratic response to Bush nuclear-arms address
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Associated Press copy reports Senate Majority Leader George Mitchell saying Democrats welcome President Bush's nuclear-threat-reduction efforts and emphasizing economic growth.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: AP Wire Story and bulletins by Terence Hunt: Bush abandons sea and land-based tactical nuclear weapons
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Associated Press urgent, bulletin, and lead-copy pages by Terence Hunt report Bush's order to eliminate land-based and sea-based tactical nuclear weapons, stand down strategic bombers, and seek Soviet reciprocal steps.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: UPI Wire Story by Bob Lewis: Bush seeks to slash nuclear arsenal
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: UPI copy by Bob Lewis reports President Bush's post-Cold War defense initiative to eliminate thousands of nuclear weapons and seek Soviet matching steps.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Reuters Wire Stories: Bush announces sweeping nuclear cuts and asks Soviets to match
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Reuters urgent and lead-copy pages report Bush's sweeping nuclear-arms reductions, deactivation of long-range land-based missiles, tactical nuclear weapon withdrawals, and call for Soviet matching steps.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Office of the Press Secretary release of President Bush's address to the nation on defense policy
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The released address from the Oval Office announces major nuclear-arms reductions, tactical nuclear weapon withdrawals, strategic bomber stand-downs, and a call for Soviet reciprocal steps.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Background briefing by Senior Administration Official on defense policy address
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A senior administration official briefs reporters on the President's nuclear-arms initiative, tactical weapons, strategic systems, sea-based weapons, missile defenses, Soviet reciprocity, budget implications, and implementation timelines.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Memorandum from Frederick D. McClure to the President re Weekly Legislative Report, October 7-11, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Frederick D. McClure briefs the President on House and Senate floor action, committee activity, appropriations, the foreign aid authorization veto threat, upcoming crime and transportation bills, and pending congressional correspondence.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential Log of Selected Congressional Mail: Week of October 4-10, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The congressional mail log summarizes selected correspondence from John Kerry, Hank Brown, Vin Weber, Claiborne Pell, Edward Feighan, Kit Bond, and Christopher Dodd.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Memorandum from Ede Holiday to the President re Cabinet Report Highlights, October 13-19, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Ede Holiday highlights the week ahead for the Cabinet, including Baker travel, Sullivan travel, economic releases, wetlands testimony, and education testimony.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Memorandum from Ede Holiday to the President re Cabinet Report, October 13-19, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Ede Holiday's Cabinet Report previews agency travel, meetings, releases, testimony, and horizon items for the week of October 13-19, 1991.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Cabinet Schedule for the Week of October 13-19, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Office of Cabinet Affairs schedule tables list Cabinet travel, meetings, testimony, fundraisers, and public appearances for October 13-19, 1991.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to Sally and Eddy re Country Music Awards
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President and Mrs. Bush thank Sally and Eddy for the Country Music Awards evening in Nashville.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to Mark re Country Music Awards
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President and Mrs. Bush thank Mark for the Country Music Awards evening and add a fishing wish.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to Naomi Judd and Wynonna Judd re Country Music Awards
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President and Mrs. Bush thank Naomi and Wynonna Judd for the Country Music Awards evening and send prayers to Naomi.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to David M. McConnell re Kennebunk High School School of Excellence pins
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President and Mrs. Bush thank David McConnell for School of Excellence pins from Kennebunk High School.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to Victor re supportive words
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks Victor for kind and supportive words and for staying in touch.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary: Friday, October 11, 1991, 1:15 P.M. EDT Update
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House News Summary update covers Anita Hill and Clarence Thomas testimony, Bush reelection steps, economic confidence, and news headlines.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press Release: Youth Development, Incorporated named the 585th Daily Point of Light
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces Youth Development, Incorporated of Albuquerque, New Mexico, as the 585th Daily Point of Light.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Message to the Senate returning S. 1722, Emergency Unemployment Compensation Act of 1991, without approval
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush returns S. 1722 without approval, arguing that the emergency unemployment compensation bill would breach the budget agreement and threaten recovery.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press Briefing by Marlin Fitzwater, October 11, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Marlin Fitzwater press briefing covers the unemployment-compensation veto, the President's schedule, the Bush-Quayle campaign committee, Judge Thomas hearings, retail prices, and the Middle East peace process.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: The Daily Briefing on American Politics: Friday, October 11, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The American Political Network Daily Briefing covers White House '92, Democratic candidates, Clarence Thomas, Casolaro, state campaigns, Senate watch, polling, and television monitoring.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Mrs. Bush's Press Office Daily Press Clippings: Friday, October 11, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Mrs. Bush's Press Office clipping packet includes coverage of Barbara Bush, Governor Sununu, Marlin Fitzwater, and related First Lady press material.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to Ricky re Country Music Awards
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President and Mrs. Bush thank Ricky for the Country Music Awards evening and for keeping the President in his prayers.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to Trisha Yearwood re Country Music Awards
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President and Mrs. Bush thank Trisha Yearwood for the Country Music Awards evening and note that country and western music will continue to play in the White House.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Pool Report #1: Going to Kansas City
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The first pool report from the November 13 trip notes the Air Force One flight to Kansas City, Senators Christopher Bond and John Danforth aboard, and travel chatter before the FFA convention stop.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Draft remarks for the FFA Convention in Kansas City, Missouri
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The draft FFA Convention remarks cover agricultural education, America 2000, rural leadership, competitiveness, capital gains, banking reform, trade, GATT, NAFTA, and post-Communist market openings.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Pool report from St. Louis to Andrews with President Bush's Cardinals response
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The return-flight pool report reproduces the pool's football note to the President, Bush's handwritten response about the Houston Oilers and St. Louis Cardinals, and a marked-up FFA speech page.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Possible questions for press availability, November 13, 1991
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The press-availability Q&A anticipates questions on Soviet humanitarian aid, Prime Minister Shamir, Saudi Arabia blood donation, foreign policy, and domestic policy.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Possible questions and answers for St. Louis affiliates interviews
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The St. Louis affiliates Q&A prepares answers on McDonnell Douglas layoffs, Zenith production, tax deductions, violent crime, HUD matters, Cochran Gardens, and education reform.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Marked draft remarks for Senator Kit Bond fundraiser in St. Louis
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The marked fundraiser draft introduces Missouri Republican figures, praises Kit Bond, attacks congressional inaction, argues for domestic and international leadership, and closes with Bond's campaign motto.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Kit Bond thank-you note and President Bush reply note after St. Louis fundraiser
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Kit Bond thanks the President for the St. Louis Celebrate America appearance, with a Bush handwritten reply thanking Bond and apologizing for missing Margaret Kelly.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Office of the Press Secretary release of President Bush's remarks at fundraiser for Senator Kit Bond
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The released remarks at Riverport Amphitheater praise Kit Bond, criticize congressional inaction, discuss capital gains, banking, crime, energy, education, transportation, trade, and foreign policy.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: George H. Walker III correspondence with President Bush re Bond event
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes President Bush's letter to Bert Walker after missing him at the Bond event, Walker's note praising the Riverport appearance, and a photocopied handwritten Bush note.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Magic Johnson AIDS Commission correspondence packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet routes and transmits President Bush's letter urging Magic Johnson to join the National AIDS Commission and offering a White House announcement if he accepts.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Ted Sanders resignation response packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes a handwritten presidential note, President Bush's formal letter accepting Ted Sanders's resignation as Deputy Secretary of Education, and Sanders's August 8 resignation letter.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Pete Domenici letter forwarded by President Bush to Frank Donatelli
directScanItemizedRecords.type: Incoming Congressional Letter with Presidential Note
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush forwards Senator Pete Domenici's letter on savings incentives and tax policy to Frank Donatelli, asking him to review Domenici's comments.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Bill Grant call note re Florida Senate race

directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces the President's intent to appoint William R. Neale as a member of the Council on Environmental Quality.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Remarks by the President to the Spring Convention of the American Society of Association Executives
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: White House press release transcript of the President's remarks to the Spring Convention of the American Society of Association Executives.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Remarks by the President and Secretary of Transportation Andrew H. Card Jr. at Card's swearing-in ceremony
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: White House press release transcript of remarks by the President and Secretary Andrew H. Card Jr. at Card's swearing-in ceremony.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House press release re appointment of David R. Clare to the President's Drug Advisory Council
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces the President's appointment of David R. Clare as a member of the President's Drug Advisory Council.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Mrs. Bush's Press Office Daily Press Clippings for Wednesday, March 11, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Mrs. Bush's Press Office clipping packet includes Houston, New York Post, Philadelphia Inquirer, USA Today, People, and Washington Post material on Barbara Bush, primaries, White House volunteers, and related coverage.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Daily News Clips packet re March 11 primaries, economy, Congress, Russia, and foreign-policy coverage
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A Daily News Clips packet collects March 11 coverage from the Washington Post, Washington Times, Wall Street Journal, USA Today, New York Times, and other outlets on Bush, Buchanan, Clinton, Tsongas, Congress, Russia, Israeli loan guarantees, Iraq, and related issues.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Photographs from the Nixon Library dinner and American Society of Association Executives remarks
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A color photograph packet shows President Bush with Richard Nixon, at the Richard Nixon Library event, in a portrait, and speaking before association executives, with related photo backs and notes.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House press release announcing Presidential Medal of Freedom award to Sam M. Walton
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces that the President will award the Presidential Medal of Freedom to Sam M. Walton.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Steeltech remarks staffing memorandum, cover memo, and Draft Three packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A White House staffing memorandum, Beth Hinchliffe cover memorandum, and Draft Three speech text prepare the President's March 16 remarks at Steeltech in Milwaukee.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential handwritten note re Sam Walton, Kathy, and Fairfield campaign material
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A From the desk of George Bush page carries the President's note about giving Sam his item, getting Kathy's material to her quickly, and a yellow note about Fairfield campaign material.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: From the President note re meeting with Senators Kasten and Lahey
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President asks Sam Skinner, Kathy Super, John Howe, and Nick Calio to arrange a close-hold Tuesday meeting with Senators Kasten and Lahey.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential remarks at Steeltech in Milwaukee, final copy
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A clean large-type copy of the President's Steeltech remarks addresses regulation, jobs, taxes, congressional action, and Steeltech's future.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential remarks to the Polish National Alliance in Chicago
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A large-type remarks copy for the Polish National Alliance in Chicago covers Polish-American ties, freedom, government, and the 1992 campaign.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Chicago fundraiser remarks draft for Hyatt Regency Bush-Quayle dinner
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A Chicago fundraiser draft for the Hyatt Regency dinner addresses Illinois supporters, Congress, jobs, family, foreign policy, and the campaign.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Milwaukee fundraiser remarks draft for Pfister Hotel event
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A Milwaukee fundraiser draft for the Pfister Hotel covers Wisconsin supporters, jobs, taxes, family, education, and the President's campaign argument.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Handwritten exchange re Nicholas Brady's National Press Club speech and economic optimism
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A Treasury Department note forwards Secretary Brady's National Press Club speech as useful economic material, with a second handwritten page recording political reaction to House and Senate prospects.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Dow Jones wire story on Brady citing retail-sales data and economic growth
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A Dow Jones story reports Secretary Nicholas Brady's argument that retail-sales data show the U.S. economy returning to a pattern of growth.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Knight-Ridder wire story on Brady, the economy, and money-supply growth
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A Knight-Ridder story reports Brady's prepared National Press Club remarks on recovery, money-supply growth, retail sales, and Bush tax policy.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Treasury News release of Secretary Nicholas F. Brady remarks to the National Press Club
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Treasury News text of Brady's National Press Club remarks argues against pessimism about America's economic future and discusses recovery.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Note to the President re Secretary Brady call and attached materials
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Rose Zamaria notes that Secretary Brady wanted to talk with the President; the handwritten response says that after calling, Brady brought over the attached material.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House note and George Bush favorites-list update packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A White House note asks the President to update a favorites list; the packet includes a marked favorites list and a George To George comparison of George Washington and George Bush favorites.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Memorandum from C. Boyden Gray re responses to congressional inaction
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: C. Boyden Gray suggests responses to congressional inaction, including pork cancellation, steel and content issues, and employee rights legislation.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Recommended telephone call sheet for Bob Collins of WGN Radio
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Bush-Quayle call sheet recommends a live March 16 WGN Radio call with Bob Collins to reach Illinois voters before the Republican primary.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Recommended telephone call sheet for J. P. McCarthy of WJR Radio
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Bush-Quayle call sheet recommends a live March 16 WJR Radio call with J. P. McCarthy to reach Michigan voters before the Republican primary.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Memorandum from Clayton Yeutter re Rio conference attendance
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Clayton Yeutter follows up on a conversation about the Rio conference, noting interagency preparations and the political risk of presidential attendance.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Memorandum from Nick Calio re Jack Russ resignation call
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Nick Calio reports Jack Russ's resignation call and the President writes instructions to call Jack and give him the President's message.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Decision note re Salinas call, Susie Brenner visit, and Ashton Phelps tennis invitation
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A yellow attached note and typed decision note ask the President about a call to President Salinas, a visit by Glen Brenner's wife Susie, and a tennis invitation from Ashton Phelps.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Media Affairs follow-up re Mrs. Brenner phone number and visit request
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Scott Sforza sends Rose Zamaria and Bridget Montagne contact guidance for Mrs. Brenner, followed by an April 1 note that Mrs. Brenner preferred not to be invited.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Marked blue copy of Salinas, Brenner, and Phelps decision note
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A blue-copy version of the March 16 decision note preserves additional handwritten markings about President Salinas, Susie Brenner, and Ashton Phelps.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Reverend Christopher Halverson prayer request response packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President sends Reverend Christopher Halverson a signed reply about the power of prayer, with the attached phone-call note explaining Halverson's request to visit with his father.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Hermogenes R. Capati condolence letter and Camp David address note
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President sends Hermogenes R. Capati condolences on the loss of his father, with an attached Camp David address and telephone note.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential note to Gerald R. Ford re support and Pat Buchanan
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks Gerald Ford for strong support and for calling on Pat Buchanan to bail out, while noting the economy, campaign, and Buchanan's divisiveness.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Pool Report #2 re motorcade to Steeltech and plant tour
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Pool Report #2 covers the President's Milwaukee motorcade to Steeltech and plant tour before his remarks.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Pool Report #1 re Andrews-to-Milwaukee flight and Air Force One Q&A
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Pool Report #1 covers the Andrews-to-Milwaukee flight and a near-verbatim Air Force One Q&A with the President.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Pool Report #3 re Milwaukee airport to the Hyatt Regency Chicago
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Pool Report #3 covers the President's movement from Milwaukee airport to the Hyatt Regency Chicago.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Remarks by the President at Bush-Quayle fundraising dinner in Chicago
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: White House press release transcript of the President's remarks at the Bush-Quayle fundraising dinner at the Hyatt Regency Chicago Hotel.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Photographs from the President's March 16 Milwaukee and Chicago trip
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A photograph packet shows the President aboard Air Force One, speaking, and attending March 16 Milwaukee and Chicago events, with official White House photograph backing labels.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Ward L. Quaal correspondence re Chicago Tribune editorial and conservative politics
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Ward L. Quaal forwards an editorial item and comments on conservative politics, with a White House address cover page and handwritten routing note.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Materials forwarded to the President, March 20, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The materials-forwarded list identifies action, classified, information, remarks, and schedule items sent to the President on March 20, including executive-order, nomination, veto, rescission, correspondence, trade, Armenia-call, and schedule material.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Dr. and Mrs. Richard Bohme economy letter response packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President replies to Dr. and Mrs. Bohme about economic anxiety and hard times; the packet includes their incoming letter, photograph page, and resume attachments.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: George Hommell World Wide Sportsman convention invitation correspondence
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President sends George Hommell a handwritten reply declining a July 15 American Fishing Tackle Manufacturers Association convention invitation because of foreign travel and schedule constraints.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Bruce C. Rounds Super Tuesday note and presidential reply
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Bruce C. Rounds congratulates the President after Super Tuesday and offers a line for Rich Bond; the President responds with a handwritten note about Florida, the award, and a speech.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Ray Scott Pintlala bass-tournament photograph distribution packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A packet of official White House photograph address pages and routing notes covers the President's Pintlala fishing event participants, including Ray Scott, Tommy Martin, Kevin Van Dam, Forrest Wood, Roland Martin, Johnny Morris, and other fishermen.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential thank-you letter to Ray Scott after Pintlala Day
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks Ray Scott for Pintlala Day and for the suggestion of fishermen's clinics, noting that campaign people will be contacted.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: From the President memorandum to Bob Teeter re Ray Scott suggestion
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President forwards Ray Scott's suggestion to Bob Teeter, asking him to consider the political value of a bass-fishing road-show idea.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Ray Scott handwritten letter proposing Bass Boys for Bush campaign work
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Ray Scott writes after the Pintlala fishing day about church, fundraising, the Bass Boys for Bush idea, fishing volunteers, and possible campaign appearances.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Typed copy of From the President memorandum to Bob Teeter re Ray Scott suggestion
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A typed copy of the President's memorandum to Bob Teeter preserves the Ray Scott suggestion forwarding language.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Draft presidential reply to Ray Scott re Pintlala Day and fishermen's clinics
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A draft reply to Ray Scott thanks him for the Pintlala outing and acknowledges his suggestion about fishermen's clinics and campaign work.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Pintlala fishing cards, address lists, and George W. Bush routing packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A routing packet collects Air Force One and Camp David notes, fishing cards, a letter to George W. Bush, and Ray Scott address lists for the President's Pintlala fishing partners and friends.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential thank-you letter to Raymond Siller re Radio and TV Correspondents dinner material
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks Raymond Siller for material used at the Radio and TV Correspondents dinner and asks that a copy of the remarks be included in the records.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Internal transcript of President's remarks at Annual Radio and Television Association Dinner
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The internal transcript records the President's March 19 remarks at the Annual Radio and Television Association Dinner at the Washington Hilton.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Raymond Siller faxed joke material for the Radio-TV Correspondents dinner
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Raymond Siller faxes suggested joke material for the Radio-TV Correspondents dinner, including a cover sheet, letter, top-ten idea, and marked-up joke pages.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Mrs. Bush's Press Office Daily Press Clippings for Friday, March 20, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Mrs. Bush's Press Office clipping packet includes New York Newsday, Washington Post, Washington Times, and other coverage of Nixon and Russia, swing voters, Hillary Clinton, Nancy Reagan, the House bank, and related political stories.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Message to the House returning H.R. 4210 without approval, duplicate copies
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Duplicate copies of the President's message to the House return H.R. 4210, the Tax Fairness and Economic Growth Acceleration Act of 1992, without approval.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Remarks by the President honoring two undefeated NCAA Division I football teams
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House press release transcript records the President's East Room remarks honoring two undefeated NCAA Division I football teams.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Executive order continuing the Presidential Service Certificate and Presidential Service Badge
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The executive order continues the Presidential Service Certificate and Presidential Service Badge for qualifying military personnel.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Statement by the Press Secretary re U.S.-Russia POW/MIA commission
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Press Secretary announces a U.S.-Russia joint commission to investigate unresolved prisoner-of-war and missing-in-action cases.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Judicial nomination announcement for Dennis G. Jacobs
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces the President's intention to nominate Dennis G. Jacobs to be United States Circuit Judge for the Second Circuit.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: National Safe Boating Week, 1992 proclamation
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The proclamation designates National Safe Boating Week, 1992, and urges Americans and governors to observe the week and learn more about boating safety.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Judicial nomination announcement for John G. Heyburn II
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces the President's intention to nominate John G. Heyburn II to be United States District Judge for the Western District of Kentucky.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Judicial nomination announcement for C. LeRoy Hansen
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces the President's intention to nominate C. LeRoy Hansen to be United States District Judge for the District of New Mexico.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Judicial nomination announcement for Richard H. Kyle
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces the President's intention to nominate Richard H. Kyle to be United States District Judge for the District of Minnesota.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Judicial nomination announcement for Gordon T. Quist
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces the President's intention to nominate Gordon T. Quist to be United States District Judge for the Western District of Michigan.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Judicial nomination announcement for Michael Boudin
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces the President's intention to nominate Michael Boudin to be United States Circuit Judge for the First Circuit.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential Determination No. 92-19 re Migration and Refugee Assistance Act
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Presidential Determination No. 92-19 makes a determination under section 2(c)(1) of the Migration and Refugee Assistance Act of 1962, as amended.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Daily Point of Light 725: Love Outreach of Richmond, Virginia
directScanItemizedRecords.type: Daily Point of Light Press Release
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House recognizes the volunteers of Love Outreach of Richmond, Virginia, as the 725th Daily Point of Light.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press release re signing courthouse naming bills for Frank M. Johnson Jr. and Ewing T. Kerr
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces the President's signing of S. 1467 and S. 1889, naming federal buildings and courthouses for Frank M. Johnson Jr. and Ewing T. Kerr.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Judicial nomination announcement for Paul L. Shechtman
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces the President's intention to nominate Paul L. Shechtman to be United States District Judge for the Southern District of New York.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Special messages transmitting 68 proposed rescissions, duplicate copies
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Duplicate copies of the White House release and proposed rescissions tables describe 67 special messages transmitting 68 rescission proposals totaling $3.6 billion in fiscal year 1992 budgetary resources.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House press release re Texas major disaster declaration
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces that the President declared a major disaster in Texas and ordered federal aid for areas struck by severe storms and flooding since March 4, 1992.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Daily Point of Light 724: My Guardian Angel of Dallas, Texas
directScanItemizedRecords.type: Daily Point of Light Press Release
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House recognizes the volunteers of My Guardian Angel of Dallas, Texas, as the 724th Daily Point of Light for peer support groups for children experiencing loss or estrangement.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Judicial nomination announcement for Justin P. Wilson
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces the President's intention to nominate Justin P. Wilson to be United States Circuit Judge for the Sixth Circuit.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Implementing the Beck Decision fact sheet, duplicate copies
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Duplicate copies of the fact sheet describe the President's steps to protect workers' rights recognized in Communications Workers of America v. Beck.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press release re signing S.J. Res. 240 for Greek Independence Day
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces the President's signing of S.J. Res. 240, designating March 25, 1992, as Greek Independence Day.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Statement by the President approving H.J. Res. 446 re enrollment requirements for H.R. 4210
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President approves H.J. Res. 446, waiving printing requirements for H.R. 4210, while reserving his view of Congress's presentation and enrollment procedures.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Major Administration Legislative Initiatives fact sheet, duplicate copies
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Duplicate copies of the fact sheet summarize major Bush Administration legislative initiatives that had not received favorable congressional action, including economic growth, education, crime, energy, banking, health, trade, and community opportunity proposals.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Statement by the Press Secretary re private dinner for Prime Minister Michael Manley
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Press Secretary announces that the President invited Jamaican Prime Minister Michael Manley to the White House for a private dinner before Manley left office.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President's Address Announcing Tax Bill Veto fact sheet, duplicate copies
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Duplicate copies of the fact sheet explain the President's tax-bill veto, seven-point economic-growth plan, Beck decision steps, regulatory reform, federal-laboratory partnerships, and global-economy strategy.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential letter to Gerald R. Ford re Cy Laughter
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President writes Gerald R. Ford about Cy Laughter, David Laughter, and Connie Horner's possible Presidential Personnel assistance.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Photographs from the President's March 20, 1992 events and Oval Office work
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A color photograph packet shows Oval Office work, a March 20 speech deadline-to-Congress scene, football-team recognition, and other presidential events and meetings from the March 20 materials packet.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Donald W. Celotto small-world report correspondence
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President replies to Connecticut Superior Court Administrative Judge Donald W. Celotto about his small-world report concerning Sandra Celano's son, a Panama veteran, with Celotto's incoming letter and a duplicate presidential reply copy.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Wanda and Jack Eidson condolence and Scott Lee Eidson AIDS/liposome correspondence packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes the President's condolence letter to Wanda and Jack Eidson, Scott Lee Eidson's earlier letters about AIDS and liposome research, Barbara Bush and White House responses, and a Louis W. Sullivan HHS reply.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Charlie Ingram Pintlala thank-you letter and photograph mailing
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks Charlie Ingram for the Pintlala fishing day and encloses official White House photographs, with address pages and mailing instructions.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Roland Martin Pintlala thank-you letter and photograph mailing
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks Roland Martin for the Pintlala fishing day and encloses a signed official White House photograph.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Orlando Wilson Pintlala thank-you letter and photograph mailing
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks Orlando Wilson for the Pintlala fishing day and encloses a signed official White House photograph.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Hank Parker Pintlala thank-you letter and photograph mailing
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks Hank Parker for the Pintlala fishing day and encloses a signed official White House photograph.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Guy Eaker Pintlala thank-you letter and photograph mailing
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks Guy Eaker for the Pintlala fishing day and encloses a set of signed official White House photographs.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Susie Peake Republican National Convention photograph routing packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet routes a presidential photograph and handwritten message to Susie Peake at the 1992 Republican National Convention office, with a Bush Library photocopy note and internal routing instructions.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Edward I. Koch correspondence re James Baker and Jewish voters
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President replies to Edward I. Koch about Secretary James Baker, Israel's security, and American Jewish political concerns; the packet includes Koch's incoming letter and New York Post and Forward clippings.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Laura A. Hanley photograph and Rose Zamaria note packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Rose Zamaria routes a photograph and note to Laura A. Hanley, executive secretary to Florida State Representative Mark Foley, after a pleasant briefing and White House photo request.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential letter to Herb Kelleher re Just Plane Terrific arm-wrestling match
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President writes Southwest Airlines chairman Herbert D. Kelleher about the Just Plane Terrific arm-wrestling match with Kurt Herwald and the comic relief it gave nightly-news viewers.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential letter to Kurt Herwald re Just Plane Terrific arm-wrestling victory
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President congratulates Stevens Aviation chairman Kurt Herwald on the arm-wrestling victory over Herb Kelleher and the company spirit shown in the Just Plane Terrific event.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Southwest-Stevens Aviation Just Plane Smart NBC transcript and clipping packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet forwards a WHCA note, NBC Nightly News transcript, Jan Baran routing note, and newspaper clipping on the Southwest-Stevens Aviation Just Plane Smart arm-wrestling settlement.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Nomination announcement for Thomas P. Kerester as Chief Counsel for Advocacy at SBA
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces the President's intention to nominate Thomas P. Kerester as Chief Counsel for Advocacy at the Small Business Administration.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Statement by the President on the ninth anniversary of the Strategic Defense Initiative
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President marks the ninth anniversary of the Strategic Defense Initiative and argues for continued missile-defense support after the Gulf War and the Missile Defense Act.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Message by the President for Nowruz, the Iranian New Year
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President sends Nowruz greetings to Iranian Americans and recognizes their contributions to American culture and public life.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Memorandum delegating responsibilities under Public Law 102-229
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President delegates responsibilities under Public Law 102-229 to the Secretaries of State and Defense and the Director of OMB, with instructions for Federal Register publication.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Text of presidential letter transmitting the 1991 Leahy-Huddleston Report
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House releases the text of the President's letter to congressional committee chairmen transmitting the classified 1991 Leahy-Huddleston Report on foreign governments' intelligence representation in the United States.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Statement by the Press Secretary re President Bush call with President Yeltsin
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Press Secretary states that President Bush briefed President Yeltsin on meetings with Chancellor Kohl and that Yeltsin discussed the CIS heads-of-state meeting, nuclear issues, and Nagorno-Karabakh.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Cancer Control Month, 1992 proclamation
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President proclaims April 1992 as Cancer Control Month and discusses cancer research, prevention, screening, diet, smoking, and public participation.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Remarks by the President at Barbara Franklin's swearing-in as Secretary of Commerce
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House press release transcript records the President's remarks and Secretary Barbara Franklin's response at her Commerce Department swearing-in ceremony.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Mrs. Bush's Press Office Daily Press Clippings for Monday, March 23, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Mrs. Bush's Press Office clipping packet includes coverage of Barbara Bush, outdoor recreation, Texas Scottish Rite Hospital, Hillary Clinton, Marilyn Quayle, political spouses, family politics, and a Paul Wylie Maalox Moment item.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Daily News Clips packet re Clinton, Brown, Kohl, Russia, trade, Perot, and March 23 coverage
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A Daily News Clips run collects Reuters, UPI, AP, New York Times, Washington Post, Wall Street Journal, USA Today, and other coverage on Bush, Clinton, Brown, Perot, Kohl, GATT, Russia, Ukraine, the House bank, the tax bill, and related political and foreign-policy stories.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential letter to William E. Simon re discretionary spending caps
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks William E. Simon for a letter passed along by Charley Bartlett and discusses the attached speech, congressional fights over discretionary spending, and keeping the 1990 budget caps in place.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential 97th birthday greeting to Guadalupe Valdez Sr.
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President and Mrs. Bush send 97th birthday greetings to Guadalupe Valdez Sr., with a handwritten note forwarding the letter through his grandson David.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: March 23 briefing and Oval Office photograph packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The photograph packet includes color prints and sticky-note labels for a BPM/Jamaica item, leading Republican Senators, a group event, domestic briefing, and Oval Office meeting scenes.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Remarks at the Youth for Tomorrow gala at the Kennedy Center
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President's prepared remarks for the Youth for Tomorrow gala discuss family strength, Joe Gibbs, Project Bridge, and the program's work with boys from troubled homes.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Prescott S. Bush Jr. abortion issue fax and presidential note packet
directScanItemizedRecords.type: Presidential Note and Family Fax Packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Prescott S. Bush Jr. faxes the President about Greenwich Magazine, abortion, Beth Bush's thoughts, and a Roger Rosenblatt quotation; the packet includes Bush's self-typed note and the Office of the President fax cover page.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Bob Teeter and Bobby Burchfield packet re Newsweek, Floyd Brown, and Citizens for Bush
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Bob Teeter forwards Bobby Burchfield's letter to Newsweek editor Maynard Parker objecting to a Floyd Brown and Citizens for Bush item and denying Bush-Quayle approval of Brown's activities.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Note from the President to Sam Skinner and Nick Calio re fetal tissue research
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President asks Sam Skinner and Nick Calio where the fetal tissue research issue stands after Senator Simpson warned that the administration position may not survive a veto fight.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Elsie Walker fax to the President re India trip and Tibetans
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Elsie Walker writes the President before departing for India, says she will check in with Peter Mulrean about the Tibetans, and notes that a copy was given to General Scowcroft.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Note from the President to Sam Skinner and Bob Teeter re weekly campaign and White House meetings
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President asks Sam Skinner and Bob Teeter to establish regular meetings with campaign and White House staff because he feels disconnected despite their updates.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Statement by the Press Secretary re trade with the former Soviet Union
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The statement announces steps to expand trade with Russia and the other new former Soviet states, including Topaz space power technology, Hall thrusters, plutonium-238, imports, exports, and COCOM controls.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Fact Sheet: Trade with former Soviet Union
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The fact sheet details administration actions on Topaz, Hall Thrusters, plutonium-238, import barriers, export licenses, COCOM controls, and expedited review of former Soviet Union trade.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Note from the President to Brent Scowcroft and Chief of Staff re U.S.-Israel relations statement
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President asks Brent Scowcroft and the Chief of Staff about responding to a Klein letter, Olmert, and a possible statement, op-ed, or speech on U.S.-Israel relations.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Note from the President to Dick Darman re Jim Miller and spending growth facts
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President asks Dick Darman for a factual response to a New York Times piece involving Jim Miller and comparative claims about federal spending growth.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Louis Connick Southeast Asia personnel correspondence packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President replies to Louis Connick about his interest in returning to Southeast Asia in an official capacity and forwards the matter to Connie Horner, with incoming material, a card, and a duplicate note.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Note from the President to Bob Teeter and Sam Skinner re conservative outreach strategy
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President asks Bob Teeter and Sam Skinner to discuss a strategy for including conservatives, mentioning Dick Allen, Tony Dolan, Bill Bennett, and Bob Tyrrell.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Note from the President to Dick Darman re entitlement caps
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President asks Dick Darman to clarify a Washington Post editorial's claim that the administration had not actually proposed capping entitlement growth.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: David Demarest memorandum re Congress Hall government reform speech
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: David Demarest sends the President the staffed draft of his April 1 government reform speech for Congress Hall in Philadelphia and flags proposed actions on Congress and executive-branch reform.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Draft presidential remarks at Old House Chamber, Philadelphia, re government reform
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Demarest/Aarhus draft frames Congress Hall as a setting for government reform, attacks entrenched Washington institutions, and proposes changes on congressional coverage, committees, campaign finance, spending, regulation, communications, nominations, and term limits.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Philip Brady memorandum re possible questions at the NAAG event
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Philip Brady sends the President material for possible response to questions at the National Association of Attorneys General event.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: NAAG event response points on crime legislation and FY 1993 crime and drug budgets
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The response points summarize Weed and Seed, the crime bill, the FY 1993 crime budget, drug budget increases, prevention and treatment funding, and statistics on declining drug use.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Zeders 50th anniversary scheduling note packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President alerts Kathy Super, Susan Rose, Patty Presock, and Mrs. Bush's office that the Zeders' 50th anniversary celebration is on August 8 and that he would like to go by if he is in town.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Remarks to the National Association of Attorneys General in the Roosevelt Room
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President's prepared remarks to state attorneys general discuss Weed and Seed, the crime bill, law enforcement, public corruption, school choice, health care reform, legal reform, and change.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential retirement letter to Anne Belcher, M.D.
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President and Mrs. Bush congratulate Anne Belcher on her retirement and salute her contributions to medicine, with a handwritten note identifying her as Suzie Munzel's mother.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Note from the President to Fred Zeder re August 8 anniversary celebration
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President writes Fred Zeder that if he is in town on August 8 he will try to attend the anniversary celebration and calls it a bash.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Note from the President to Robert H. Simon re Cormac thanks
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President sends a handwritten thank-you note to Robert H. Simon of the White House Speechwriting Office, saying Cormac thanks him and that even the rotund one thanks him.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Charles Brandts TV cabinet thank-you letter and handwritten draft
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks White House carpenter Charles Brandts for the bedroom TV cabinet, with the final letter and Bush's handwritten draft asking staff to get the first name of the craftsman.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Peggy Dolan Connecticut Bush campaign clipping packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet sends Peggy Dolan, with a blind copy to Tony Dolan, a clipping on Connecticut Bush backers and the Republican delegate fight.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Anthony R. Dolan cover letter to the President re campaign strategy memo
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Anthony R. Dolan tells the President he is sending a nine-page post-New Hampshire strategy memorandum that still deals with campaign problems and opportunities.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Anthony R. Dolan memorandum to George Bush re conservatives and campaign strategy
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Dolan's memorandum argues that conservatives remain both a problem and an opportunity after New Hampshire, sketches sample campaign coverage, urges a conservative strategy, and recommends personnel and volunteer moves.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Note from the President to S. Michele Nix re Cormac thanks
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President sends S. Michele Nix of the White House Speechwriting Office a handwritten note saying Cormac, the noble one, sends sincere thanks and that it worked swell.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Charles L. Bartlett Sunday luncheon guest-list fax and presidential note
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes Charley Bartlett's faxed Sunday luncheon guest list for the President and Mrs. Bush and Bush's handwritten note to Martha Bartlett about the event, Robert Maxwell, and Barbara Bush.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Paul L. Hubbard and New Detroit packet re Chris McAllister
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: New Detroit president Paul L. Hubbard thanks the President for his Economic Club of Detroit speech and for recognizing Chris McAllister's question, with presidential handwriting and address pages.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Nomination announcement re Charles W. Larson as U.S. Attorney for the Northern District of Iowa
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces the President's intention to reappoint Charles W. Larson as United States Attorney for the Northern District of Iowa.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Daily Point of Light 732: Richard Hassell of Norfolk, Virginia
directScanItemizedRecords.type: Daily Point of Light Press Release
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House recognizes Richard Hassell of Norfolk, Virginia, as the 732nd Daily Point of Light for Christian Action and more than 50 years of service to neighbors and people in poverty.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Statement by Deputy Press Secretary Judy Smith re President Bush meeting with President George Vassiliou
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Judy Smith states that President Bush met with President George Vassiliou of Cyprus and discussed the UN-led effort to negotiate a fair and permanent settlement to the Cyprus dispute.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Judicial nomination announcement for Loretta A. Preska
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces the President's intention to nominate Loretta A. Preska as United States District Judge for the Southern District of New York.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Nomination announcement re Jay D. Gardner as U.S. Attorney for the Southern District of Georgia
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces the President's intention to nominate Jay D. Gardner as United States Attorney for the Southern District of Georgia.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Remarks by President Bush and President George Vassiliou during Oval Office photo opportunity
