directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The press release transcript records President Bush and President George Vassiliou answering questions about Cyprus, the UN process, Turkey, Soviet aid, and the U.S. election year.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Daily News Clips packet re Clinton, Brown, Russia, health care, Congress, Perot, and March 30 coverage
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Office of Media Affairs Daily News Clips packet collects March 30 coverage from the New York Times, Washington Post, Washington Times, Wall Street Journal, USA Today, wire services, and other outlets on the campaign, Russia, health care, House bank, Israel, Beck, Perot, and related political and policy stories.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: March 30 Youth for Tomorrow gala and economic briefing photograph packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A color photograph packet shows the President at the Youth for Tomorrow gala and in an Oval Office economic briefing sequence, including a yellow sticky-note label on the briefing photographs.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Recommended telephone call to Congressman Tim Penny re budget firewalls bill
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Nicholas Calio recommends that the President call Congressman Tim Penny to thank him for helping defeat H.R. 3732, the Budget Process Reform Act, known as the firewalls bill.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Constance Horner note to the President re Digger Phelps and ONDCP
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Constance Horner tells the President that Digger Phelps is renegotiating his Notre Dame contract so he can come on board at the Office of National Drug Control Policy.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Points for briefing with congressional leaders re former Soviet Union assistance program
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President's briefing points ask congressional leaders to support a comprehensive assistance program for Russia and other former Soviet states, including IMF, G-7, Freedom Support Act, CCC credit, exchange, demilitarization, and trade measures.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Earth Summit Oval Office briefing
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The revised briefing sheet sets the purpose, background, participants, press plan, sequence, and no-remarks plan for an Oval Office Earth Summit briefing with Clayton Yeutter and senior officials.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Ronald C. Kaufman memorandum re Vermont caucus results
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Ronald Kaufman reports the President's Vermont caucus victory, describes Buchanan's weak presence and Brown's Democratic result, and attaches Republican and Democratic caucus-result figures.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Vermont caucus thank-you letter packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes signed and duplicate presidential thank-you letters to John Lindley III, James H. Douglas, Jeffrey Amestoy, and Allen Martin for helping place the Vermont caucus in the win column.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Robin Higgins Presidential Citizens Medal correspondence packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President replies to Robin L. Higgins after she thanked him and Mrs. Bush for the Presidential Citizens Medal awarded to her husband Rich and for remembering his sacrifice.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Note from the President to Dan Burton re Kanjorski and White House travel on television
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks Dan Burton for a television appearance that put Paul Kanjorski's moves into proper perspective and sends the note by courier.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential thank-you letter to Debby Graham re handcrafted stamp holder
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks Debby Graham for a handcrafted stamp holder sent to him and Mrs. Bush and wishes her luck with future creative endeavors.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Dan Jenkins correspondence packet re press pounding and campaign outlook
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President replies to Dan Jenkins about the rough political pounding, the improving economy, golf, gratitude, family, and friendship, with Jenkins's March 24 incoming letter and a duplicate presidential copy.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: James E. Plowman law school recommendation letters
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President writes recommendation letters for James E. Plowman to George Mason University School of Law dean Henry Manne and George Washington University president Stephen Trachtenberg.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Note from the President to Kitty and David Czarnecki re White House evening
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President writes Kitty and David Czarnecki that he will send photos of their evening at the White House and thanks them for a note and a superb performance.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Chana Shwadlenak note and Cimarron presidential visit clipping packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President sends Chana Shwadlenak a handwritten note, with routing to Andy Foster and Rose Zamaria, and the packet includes a Cimarron article on students enjoying the President's Oklahoma Christian University visit.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Jeb Bush and Bush Klein Realty Miami supporter photograph autograph and lapel pin packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet routes signed presidential photographs and lapel pins through Jeb Bush and Bush Klein Realty for Miami supporters including Roberto Suarez, Ruben Mendiola, Jorge Luis Lopez, Constance Gubitosi, Carlos Salman, Gilberto Wong, Arthur Teele, Eliseo and Lucrecia Machado, Paul Douglas, Sherman Winn, and Paul and Carmen Foster.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Maryam Sachs Der Kuss book correspondence packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks Maryam Sachs for her letter and for Der Kuss, noting its photography and asking that the book be logged and sent to the Residence.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Mrs. Bush's Press Office Daily Press Clippings for Wednesday, April 1, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Mrs. Bush's Press Office clipping packet includes distribution cover and coverage of the First Lady, Bush travel and perks, Marilyn Quayle, Cuba, Laura Dern, and related public-life items.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Statement by the President on approving H.J. Res. 456
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President announces approval of H.J. Res. 456, which funds economic and democratic development assistance for former Soviet republics, international agencies, disaster loans, and UN peacekeeping activities.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Remarks by joint congressional members after meeting with the President re former Soviet Union assistance
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The internal transcript records Senators Mitchell, Pell, Helms, Dole and Representatives Gephardt and Fascell discussing the President's former Soviet Union assistance package after a White House meeting.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Judicial nomination announcement for Alfred V. Covello
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces the President's intention to nominate Alfred V. Covello as United States District Judge for the District of Connecticut.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Message to Congress re Hungary and Czechoslovakia emigration criteria and MFN status
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President transmits an updated report on Hungary and Czechoslovakia emigration laws and policies and states that the administration is taking steps to terminate title IV Trade Act application to both countries.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Judicial nomination announcement for Irene M. Keeley
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces the President's intention to nominate Irene M. Keeley as United States District Judge for the Northern District of West Virginia.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Judicial nomination announcement for Carol E. Jackson
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces the President's intention to nominate Carol E. Jackson as United States District Judge for the Eastern District of Missouri.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Judicial nomination announcement for Charles A. Banks
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces the President's intention to nominate Charles A. Banks as United States District Judge for the Eastern District of Arkansas.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Judicial nomination announcement for Jerome B. Simandle
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces the President's intention to nominate Jerome B. Simandle as United States District Judge for the District of New Jersey.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Agricultural Assistance for the NIS press release
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The release announces $1.1 billion in additional GSM-102 credit guarantees for U.S. agricultural commodity purchases by Russia, Ukraine, Armenia, and other independent states of the former Soviet Union.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Daily Point of Light 734: St. Mary's Interfaith Dining Room of Stockton, California
directScanItemizedRecords.type: Daily Point of Light Press Release
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House recognizes volunteers of St. Mary's Interfaith Dining Room of Stockton, California, as the 734th Daily Point of Light for feeding the hungry and serving homeless and indigent people.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Statement by the Press Secretary re intelligence community program and organization changes
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The statement says the President approved intelligence-community program, resource, structure, management, collection, and military-support changes after a comprehensive requirements review.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Multilateral Financial Assistance Package for Russia press release
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The release describes U.S. support for roughly $18 billion in financial support for Russian reform, a $6 billion currency stabilization fund, and early IMF membership for Russia and other new states.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Message to the Senate transmitting Nigeria mutual legal assistance treaty
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President transmits the U.S.-Nigeria Mutual Legal Assistance in Criminal Matters treaty to the Senate and recommends early favorable consideration and consent to ratification.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Freedom Support Act of 1992 fact sheet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The fact sheet explains the Freedom Support Act's framework for humanitarian aid, nuclear safety, demilitarization, free markets, democracy, food credits, trade and investment, IMF support, stabilization funds, and people-to-people contacts.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press conference by the President and Secretaries Baker, Brady, and Madigan re former Soviet Union assistance, duplicate copies
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Duplicate transcript copies record the President, James Baker, Nicholas Brady, and Edward Madigan discussing the Russia and former Soviet Union assistance package, Freedom Support Act, agricultural credits, IMF support, G-7 funding, and questions about cost.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Daily News Clips packet re Russia aid, Clinton, Brown, Perot, House perks, courts, and April 1 coverage
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Office of Media Affairs Daily News Clips packet collects April 1 coverage from USA Today, the Wall Street Journal, the New York Times, the Washington Post, the Washington Times, and other outlets on Russia aid, the campaign, House perks, women voters, defense, courts, Iran-Contra, and Earth Summit politics.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential 50th anniversary letter to George and Mrs. Wessel
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President and Mrs. Bush congratulate George Wessel Jr. and Mrs. Wessel on their 50th wedding anniversary, with a handwritten instruction asking Patty to place the original.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: April 1 photograph packet re bipartisan congressional leadership, phone call, and Oval Office meetings
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A color photograph packet includes Oval Office and meeting photographs, sticky-note labels for bipartisan congressional leadership and a phone call, and official White House photograph identifiers.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Cathy Turner signed Olympic photograph and routing note packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes a photograph inscribed by the President to Olympic gold medalist Cathy Turner and a presidential note asking Dave Valdez and Susan Biddle for a hugging shot to sign and send.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Governors' Ethanol Coalition response and incoming-letter packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President replies to Governors' Ethanol Coalition chairman Ben Nelson about ethanol in reformulated gasoline, with clearance materials, a draft response, and Nelson's March 6 incoming letter on EPA implementation of the Clean Air Act.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Tennis participants clearance note for April 8, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Bridget tells Rose Zamaria that the President, David Bates, Dorrance Smith, and Steve Caruthers have been cleared for tennis and parking.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential phone call slip with Arthur Ashe re AIDS
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A presidential phone-call slip records an April 8 call with Arthur Ashe concerning AIDS and the President's handwritten notes about a pleasant call after Ashe's announcement.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: RNC Eagles reception briefing, talking points, and duplicate sequence pages
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The RNC Eagles packet includes the State Floor reception briefing, political talking points, and repeated Social Office sequence pages for the President and Mrs. Bush's April 8 reception with Republican National Committee Eagles.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: U.S. Winter Olympics Rose Garden final card remarks, duplicate copies
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Duplicate card-format remarks for the South Lawn or Rose Garden event salute the 1992 U.S. Olympic Winter Team, including Kristi Yamaguchi, Bonnie Blair, Cathy Turner, Donna Weinbrecht, Hilary Lindh, Nancy Kerrigan, and other athletes.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: The President's unpublished schedule for April 8, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The schedule packet lists the President's April 8 schedule, including staff time, the Great American Read-Aloud Day event, meetings, a photo opportunity, the U.S. Olympic Winter Team ceremony, and the RNC Eagles reception.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Harry the Dirty Dog reading copy for Great American Read-Aloud Day
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes a photocopied reading copy of Gene Zion and Margaret Bloy Graham's Harry the Dirty Dog, prepared for the President's Read-Aloud Day appearance with first graders.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Great American Read-Aloud Day pre-brief and Joshua Burgess correspondence packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The pre-brief explains the President's 7:50 a.m. Oval Office briefing for Read-Aloud Day and attaches Joshua Barney Burgess's note and a photo/mailing request for a signed presidential photograph.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Great American Read-Aloud Day event briefing and talking points
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The event briefing and talking points prepare the President to read Harry the Dirty Dog to St. Peter's Interparish School first graders in the Diplomatic Reception Room and to recognize Joshua Burgess.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Meeting with William T. Ylvisaker briefing
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Constance Horner briefs the President on an Oval Office meeting with William T. Ylvisaker, who had declined the Saudi Arabia ambassadorial post but remained a possible administration appointee.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Photo opportunity with departing Postmaster General Anthony Frank briefing
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Ede Holiday briefs the President for an Oval Office photograph with departing Postmaster General Tony Frank after Frank's resignation and return to California.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Ceremony for U.S. Olympic Athletes event briefing
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The ceremony briefing prepares the President to congratulate the 1992 U.S. Olympic Winter Team, receive team jackets from gold medalists, and note Digger Phelps's appointment to ONDCP.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Richard D. Wood Saudi ambassador correspondence and routing note packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President writes Eli Lilly chairman Richard D. Wood after Wood declines the Saudi Arabia ambassadorial post, with Wood's incoming letter and a presidential note asking Constance Horner to keep Wood in mind.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Draft-three remarks clearance packet for reception for U.S. Olympic athletes
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The remarks clearance packet includes a White House staffing memorandum, Curt Smith cover memorandum, and draft-three text for the President's April 8 remarks to U.S. Olympic athletes.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary for Wednesday, April 8, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Office of the Press Secretary's White House News Summary covers primary results, Democratic campaign coverage, Jewish activists and Bush, the economy, Arafat, Israel, editorials, columns, and network-news items.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Max Fisher returned-call note
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A staff note tells the President that Max Fisher returned his call at 2:45 p.m. and that the White House has a number available to replace the call.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Secretary Mosbacher return-call request
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Robin informs the President that Secretary Mosbacher asked for a return call at the President's convenience and would be in his office.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Ronald C. Kaufman memorandum re New York, Kansas, Minnesota, and Wisconsin primary results
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Ronald Kaufman reports that the President has 1,049 delegates after the April 7 primaries and attaches state-by-state Republican and Democratic results for New York, Kansas, Minnesota, and Wisconsin.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Ken Lay Rio Conference and natural gas letter packet
directScanItemizedRecords.type: Presidential Note and Incoming Letter Packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President forwards Kenneth L. Lay's April 3 Enron letter to Clayton Yeutter as an FYI; Lay urges attendance at the Rio Conference and a market-based approach to greenhouse-gas stabilization and natural-gas use.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Jack Stein, Tom Pickering, and U.S.-Israel relations correspondence and notes packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes a signed presidential letter to Jacob Stein honoring Tom Pickering, a typed presidential memorandum to Sam Skinner and Brent Scowcroft on Stein's U.S.-Israel suggestions, and handwritten draft notes on Rabin, Israel Independence Day, Passover, and Jewish organizational outreach.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential phone call slip re Teeter tax-overpayment issue
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A presidential phone-call slip records an April 8 call with Teeter concerning a tax-overpayment issue, with handwritten notes about cancellation and coming to the office.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential phone call slip with Nicholas Brady re EBRD meeting and growth in Europe
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A presidential phone-call slip records an April 8 call with Nicholas Brady about an EBRD meeting in Budapest, contact with Beregovoy, and discussion of growth in Europe.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: FERC natural gas pipeline restructuring statement clearance packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes the President's statement on Federal Energy Regulatory Commission natural gas pipeline reforms and Phillip Brady's note seeking release of the statement that afternoon.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: National Medal of the Arts decision memorandum re Minnie Pearl and Earl Scruggs
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Phillip Brady asks the President how to handle Minnie Pearl's National Medal of the Arts after her stroke, with options and attached background on Earl Scruggs as an alternate recipient.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential phone call slip with Terri Lacy re taxes
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A presidential phone-call slip records an April 8 call with Terri Lacy about taxes and notes a follow-up meeting still set for 2:30 on Monday.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: APN Hotline Daily Briefing on American Politics for April 8, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The American Political Network Hotline briefing covers Clinton, Tsongas, Brown, Bush primary results, independent ballot access, Senate races, polls, television monitoring, and press commentary after the April 7 primaries.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Little League International Congress video remarks
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The video remarks salute Little League participants, families, and volunteers at the 1992 International Congress and connect the program to civic responsibility and community life.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: AARP 1992 Biennial Convention video remarks
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The video remarks address AARP members at the 1992 Biennial Convention and emphasize older Americans' service, independence, and place in the national community.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Overseas Private Investment Corporation video remarks
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The video remarks discuss the Overseas Private Investment Corporation and the role of private investment, enterprise, and American business in post-Cold War international development.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Christopher Columbus Quincentenary Jubilee Commission Foundation gala video remarks
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The video remarks address a Quincentennial Foundation gala and frame the Columbus anniversary around exploration, national heritage, and cultural commemoration.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Congressman John Paul Hammerschmidt video remarks
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The video remarks honor Congressman John Paul Hammerschmidt's public service, Arkansas career, and work with the President in Congress.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Armed Forces Day message
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The message marks Armed Forces Day by recognizing military service, national defense, and the contribution of servicemembers and their families.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Memorial Day message
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The message prepares Memorial Day language honoring Americans who died in military service and connecting remembrance to national obligation.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House staffing memorandum and Brady cover memo for April 9 videotaping session
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet routes the President's April 9 videotaping session and attaches six video scripts, with Phillip Brady noting the scripts and changes for the President's review.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Draft-two AARP Biennial Convention video script
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A draft-two version of the AARP video script refines the President's message for the 1992 Biennial Convention.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Draft-two John Paul Hammerschmidt video script
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A draft-two version of the Hammerschmidt video script provides revised tribute remarks for Congressman John Paul Hammerschmidt.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Draft-two Overseas Private Investment Corporation video script
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A draft-two OPIC script refines the President's video message on private investment and American economic engagement abroad.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Draft-two Quincentennial Foundation gala video script
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A draft-two script revises the President's video remarks for the Christopher Columbus Quincentenary Foundation gala.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Draft-two Little League International Congress video script
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A draft-two Little League script refines the President's remarks for the International Congress video message.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: American Society of Newspaper Editors address
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The address to the American Society of Newspaper Editors discusses the post-Cold War order, Russia and former Soviet Union assistance, trade, fiscal policy, and the United States' international role.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: John M. Lindley III Vermont flood-aid correspondence, signed photograph, and incoming-letter packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes the President's signed reply to John M. Lindley III, a signed photograph, Lindley's handwritten transmittal note, and his March 25 letter thanking the administration for federal aid after Vermont flooding.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Shirley Green memorandum re Mail Summary, April 1-7, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Shirley Green summarizes White House correspondence received and processed for April 1-7, including economic concerns, Israel loan guarantees, abortion, the Earth Summit, hardship cases, and propaganda mail.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: APN Hotline Daily Briefing on American Politics for April 9, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The American Political Network Hotline briefing compiles April 9 political intelligence on the presidential campaign, congressional races, state contests, polls, television monitoring, and press commentary.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Duplicate presidential phone-call slips with Nicholas Brady re interest-rate move and market reaction
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Duplicate presidential phone-call slips record a 12:35 p.m. April 9 call with Nicholas Brady concerning an interest-rate move and the market reaction, with handwritten notes on the call form.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential handwritten notes re Houston Chronicle, Andy Card, and school-story coverage
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President's handwritten notes discuss Houston Chronicle writer Greg McDonald, Andy Card, and the press handling of a school-story reaction account.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Carlos Andres Perez Peru and Haiti telephone-call packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes a withdrawal sheet for withheld presidential phone-call notes, points to be made for a call with Venezuelan President Carlos Andres Perez, a Brent Scowcroft cover memorandum, and background on Peru and Haiti.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Meeting with Senator Steve Symms briefing re Taiwan
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The briefing prepares the President for an April 9 meeting with Senator Steve Symms of Idaho and focuses on Taiwan, arms-sales politics, and related foreign-policy concerns.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: John M. Walker Jr. family correspondence packet re Katherine
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes the President's reply to Judge John M. Walker Jr. and Walker's handwritten incoming letter discussing family news and Katherine.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Alex Kozinski article, The Dark Lessons of Utopia
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The February 1992 American Institute for Economic Research bulletin reprints Alex Kozinski's article on collectivism, Eastern Europe, markets, and the lessons of communist rule.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Recommended telephone calls to Senators Grassley and Jeffords re Exon amendment
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Nicholas Calio recommends calls to Senators Chuck Grassley and Jim Jeffords urging votes against the Exon amendment to the Senate Budget Resolution, which would cut defense below the President's request.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Talking points for meeting with law enforcement leaders
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The talking points prepare the President for an April 13 Roosevelt Room meeting with law enforcement leaders, covering Operation Weed and Seed, crime legislation, habeas reform, the exclusionary rule, and federal-local law enforcement partnership.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary 10:15 A.M. News Update for April 13, 1992, duplicate copies
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Duplicate copies of the White House News Summary update cover Libya and Pan Am 103 sanctions, Peru and the OAS, Yeltsin's government, Uruguay Round trade talks, and Japan's trade surplus.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Beck executive order signing Rose Garden remarks
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Rose Garden remarks prepare the President to sign an executive order implementing the Beck decision for federal contractors, addressing union dues, worker political rights, Thomas Jefferson's birthday, and Charlton Heston's support.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Michael Manley correspondence and presidential routing-note packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes Michael Manley's April 2 letter to the President about a farewell dinner, regional trade and debt, medical leave, campaign wishes, and family regards, with duplicate copy and a presidential routing note to Doro, Jeb, and the NSC.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: APN Hotline Daily Briefing on American Politics for April 13, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The American Political Network Hotline briefing compiles April 13 political intelligence on Clinton, Brown, Perot, Buchanan, Bush versus Clinton, labor, media critiques, Senate races, House retirements, polling, and television monitoring.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: George Bell and Walter Annenberg correspondence re press responsibility
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: George Bell writes from Sunnylands praising the President's response to Walter Annenberg's advice, and Annenberg adds a faxed note about press responsibility and power without responsibility.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Dick Allen conservative campaign recommendations and follow-up packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet tracks follow-up on Dick Allen's conservative campaign recommendations, including a Robert Mosbacher memorandum about Martin Anderson and helpful economists, presidential handwritten follow-up notes, Rose's routing note, and the ten recommendations for conservatives in 1992.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Newt Gingrich memorandum re Social Security earnings limit
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Newt Gingrich warns Sam Skinner that the House Republican Party is likely to endorse full repeal of the Social Security earnings limit and urges that the President not publicly oppose that platform position.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Bush-Quayle 1992 research fax re Governor Clinton's tax returns, duplicate copies
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Duplicate Bush-Quayle 1992 fax packets send Sam Skinner, for the President's review, David Tell's research memorandum and attachments on Governor Clinton's tax-return disclosure history and related Arkansas coverage.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential note to Sam Skinner and Bob Teeter re defense-worker and servicemember transition commission
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President asks Sam Skinner and Bob Teeter about creating a bipartisan blue-ribbon commission for laid-off defense industry workers and servicemembers facing a tough job market after leaving the service.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Forwarding note from the President to Sam Skinner re Carter malaise speech
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: From Walker's Point, the President forwards Jimmy Carter's 1979 energy and national goals address to Sam Skinner, with copies to James Watkins, Bob Teeter, and Dave Demarest, warning against the kind of handwringing Carter conveyed.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential Documents reprint of Jimmy Carter's Energy and National Goals address
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The reprint reproduces President Jimmy Carter's July 15, 1979 address on energy and national goals, including the confidence crisis, dependence on foreign oil, conservation, and his proposed energy program.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Note from the President to Ron Kaufman re Billy Graham and the Republican convention
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President tells Ron Kaufman, with copies to Craig Fuller and Bob Teeter, not to pressure Billy Graham about appearing at the convention.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press column by George J. Mitchell on campaign-finance reform
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A newspaper clipping by George J. Mitchell discusses House and Senate action on campaign-finance reform legislation.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Note from the President to Ron Kaufman re Jon Bush, Al D'Amato, and Victory '92
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President reports that Jon Bush called at Al D'Amato's behest in favor of a joint D'Amato-Victory '92 fundraiser and asks Kaufman to advise the campaign.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Note from the President to Sam Skinner re Rosty and Chicago
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks Sam Skinner for a prompt response to Chicago's troubles and notes Dan Rostenkowski's request to talk money for Chicago.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Telephone memoranda and Signal Switchboard log: April 17, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: White House telephone memoranda list calls with Billy Graham, Gordon Zacks, Max Fisher, Roger Ailes, George W. Bush, Brent Scowcroft, Sam Skinner, and others.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential Movements: Kennebunkport, Maine, April 17, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The movement log records low-key motorcade movements between Walker's Point, North Street Congregational Church, and Kenneth Raynor's residence.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary: Friday, April 17, 1992, 6 A.M. EDT Edition
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Office of the Press Secretary news summary opens with education-plan coverage, Clinton economic criticism, Perot ballot coverage, abortion counseling, Bosnia, Libya, Iraq, and network news.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Political cartoon packet on the 1992 campaign and House Bank scandal
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A packet of clipped political cartoons covers Clinton, Perot, Iran, Boris Yeltsin, House Bank criticism, unemployment, and other campaign subjects.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to Bud Walton re Sam Walton
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President sends condolences to Bud Walton after Sam Walton's death and recalls his Bentonville visit before Walton's passing.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to Rob Walton re Sam Walton
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks Rob Walton for his hospitality in Bentonville and sends condolences after Sam Walton's death.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: The Daily Briefing on American Politics: Friday, April 17, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The American Political Network briefing summarizes Bush and Clinton education/economic coverage, Perot, House Bank fallout, polling, Senate races, and TV monitoring.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Materials Forwarded to the President: April 17, 1992, to Kennebunkport
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The forwarded-materials list identifies action, classified, information, remarks, and schedule items, including Cuba policy, Canada beer, a Cabinet report, and an Ohio trip notebook.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press Release: Kristen Wunderle recognized as the 749th Daily Point of Light
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces Kristen Wunderle of Parma, Ohio, as the 749th Daily Point of Light for her work with residents of the Corinthian Nursing Home.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Pool Report: Dinner for Six
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Frank Murray reports on President and Mrs. Bush's dinner at Ken Raynor's Kennebunk residence.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Pool Report #3: The Bushes Attend Good Friday Services
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: John Yang reports on President and Mrs. Bush attending Good Friday services at First Congregational Church in Kennebunkport.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to George and Sandy Dunagin
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks Mr. and Mrs. Dunagin for support and encloses a signed photograph.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from George, Sandy, and Angela Dunagin to President Bush
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Dunagin family writes after Super Tuesday to encourage President Bush, praise his record, and request an autographed photograph.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Mrs. Bush's Press Office Daily Press Clippings: Friday, April 17, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Mrs. Bush's Press Office clipping packet covers Barbara Bush's Los Angeles Mission visit, Millie's Book royalties, Hillary Clinton, and working women.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Information Memorandum from Brent Scowcroft to the President re New Presidential Initiative on Cuba
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Brent Scowcroft outlines a plan for a presidential Cuba statement, Torricelli bill modifications, contacts with Mack and Ros-Lehtinen, and a follow-up letter to interested members.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Schedules of the President: April 17-19, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The schedule pages list the President in Kennebunkport for Good Friday, Saturday, and Easter Sunday.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential phone-call slips and handwritten notes packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A Bush Library photocopy packet includes From the Desk of George Bush pages, handwritten notes, presidential phone-call slips, and a message from Rose Zamaria about George DePontis and Luz Santa Romana.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Information Memorandum from Brent Scowcroft to the President re Update on TV Marti
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Brent Scowcroft reports that Cuban test-pattern broadcasting interfered with TV Marti and summarizes agency disagreement over whether to broadcast over the Cuban signal.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Memorandum for Sam Skinner re possible withdrawal of OCS tracts from the 1992-97 leasing program
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A memorandum discusses whether to exempt 63 Outer Continental Shelf tracts off the Santa Barbara/San Luis Obispo coast from Interior's 1992-97 leasing program, with a handwritten presidential note at the top.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Lions International 75th Anniversary invitation correspondence packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes Robert K. Turner, Kathy Super, Norman Dahl, Donald Banker, and handwritten material regarding a proposed presidential appearance at the Lions International 75th Anniversary banquet.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: FERC Order No. 636 news release and fact sheet on natural gas pipeline restructuring
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Federal Energy Regulatory Commission materials announce and summarize Order No. 636, the Restructuring Rule for interstate natural gas pipelines.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Don Ritter correspondence and handwritten notes re quality and American competitiveness
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A packet addressed to Representative Don Ritter includes handwritten material discussing quality, competitiveness, administration beacons, and related policy arguments.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Faxed report by A. V. Feigenbaum: Quality and America's Competitiveness
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: General Systems Company faxed A. V. Feigenbaum's April 1992 essay on quality and America's competitiveness for Representative Don Ritter.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: TV reports and clipping material on Barbara Bush's Los Angeles Mission visit
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: TV Reports material for the Social Office summarizes coverage of Barbara Bush visiting and dedicating the Los Angeles Mission Education Center.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Newstab wire reports on Mark Russell, Ross Perot polling, AMVETS awards, and Points of Light
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Newstab items from April 18-20 cover Mark Russell jokes, Ross Perot polling, AMVETS Silver Helmet awards, and Columbia Savings receiving a Points of Light award.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Loose press clippings on Bush family business, Mary Matalin, Millie, Marilyn Quayle, Hillary Clinton, and women in politics
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Loose clippings include Jeff Gerth on Bush relatives' business dealings, Mary Matalin profiles, Millie's Book royalties, Marilyn Quayle coverage, Hillary Clinton analysis, and op-eds on women in politics.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Mrs. Bush's Press Office Daily Press Clippings: April 18-21, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Mrs. Bush's Press Office clipping packet covers Barbara Bush's campaign role, the President and Mrs. Bush in Kennebunkport, AmeriFlora, the White House Easter Egg Roll, and Barbara Bush's Pepperdine and Los Angeles Mission appearances.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from Rev. James L. Lufkin inviting the Bushes to Easter sunrise service
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Rev. James L. Lufkin of the Village Baptist Church invites President and Mrs. Bush to an Easter Sunday sunrise service on the rocks at Walker Point.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from Rev. Paul A. Johnson inviting the Bushes to Kennebunk Easter sunrise service
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Rev. Paul A. Johnson writes that the Kennebunk Area Clergy Association will sponsor its annual Easter Sunrise Service at Narragansett Point and invites the Bush family to attend or send a greeting.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Note from the President to Brent Scowcroft re Shamir attacks about Arabs
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President tells Brent Scowcroft that Yitzhak Shamir had upped the ante by attacking him on a deal with the Arabs and asks whether he should blast him.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Note from the President to Sam Skinner and Bob Teeter re network debate schedule
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President reports Roger Ailes's advice that the campaign announce a debate schedule, one for each network, and say two debates are enough, with copies to Dorrance and Marlin.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Note from the President to Kathy Super re Roger Ailes and Rush Limbaugh
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President asks Kathy Super to have Roger Ailes bring in Rush Limbaugh for a short meeting, noting Ailes will be producing a Rush television show and asking Sam Skinner and Marlin Fitzwater to clear any campaign concerns.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Note from the President to Bob Teeter and Sam Skinner re Roger Ailes and speech themes
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President asks Bob Teeter and Sam Skinner to keep Roger Ailes advised of major speech themes such as five pillars and education goals.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Note from the President to Boyden Gray re Biden letter and judicial backlog
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President sends Boyden Gray a note saying Biden is setting the administration up in a letter by placing blame for the judicial backlog on the White House.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Congressional Monitor for the weeks of April 10-16, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The monitor summarizes member correspondence on Saudi arms sales, judicial vacancies, ethanol, and railroad employees, including items from Mel Levine, Joseph Biden, Bob Dole, and Chris Whittle.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Telephone memoranda and Signal Switchboard log: April 18, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: White House telephone memoranda list April 18 Kennebunkport calls through the Signal Switchboard and President's line, including Kenneth Raynor and William Farish.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential Movements: Kennebunkport, Maine, April 18, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The movement log records the President's low-key motorcade from Walker's Point to Goose Rocks Beach and back on April 18, 1992.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Radio address remarks on Job Training 2000
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The embargoed radio address remarks discuss Job Training 2000, Lifetime Education and Training Accounts, skill standards, and opportunity for education and work.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Statement by the President re Cuba and the Cuban Democracy Act
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President backs a stronger Cuban Democracy Act, calls for tightening the embargo and preserving humanitarian channels, and announces Treasury action restricting vessels engaged in trade with Cuba.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Pool Report #5: Bush walks very fast at Goose Rocks Beach
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Pool Report #5 covers the President's fast power walk at Goose Rocks Beach, comments on Saudi arms-transfer allegations, local demonstrators, Roger Clemens, the Texas Rangers, and storm damage at Walker's Point.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Whistling Wings Farm raspberry jam welcome-home gift correspondence packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes the President's handwritten note to Fred Conti about an embroidered shirt, a thank-you letter to Don and Julie Harper for a welcome-home gift of raspberry jams and toppings, Whistling Wings Farm envelope and card material, Fred Conti's Initially Yours card, and a Port Video receipt.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Photograph and presidential note to Ted Cohen re Cape Arundel tee shot
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: An upside-down photocopied photograph bears the President's handwritten note to Ted Cohen warning about a shanked tee shot at Cape Arundel, with an additional note thanking Cohen and mentioning Tom Raum.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Situation Room transmittal for AmeriFlora presidential remarks packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House Situation Room transmits the AmeriFlora presidential remarks packet by immediate precedence on April 16, 1992.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Curt Smith memorandum to the President re AmeriFlora 1992 remarks
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Curt Smith briefs the President on the AmeriFlora '92 international flower show in Columbus, noting Bob Hope's introduction, the First Lady's honorary role, Columbus themes, and the remarks' discovery and open-trade message.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Draft Three presidential remarks for AmeriFlora in Columbus
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Draft Three of the AmeriFlora remarks thanks Bob Hope, Governor Voinovich, Mayor Lashutka, and other hosts while linking Columbus, discovery, an open world, trade, and America 2000 themes.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Situation Room transmittal for AmeriFlora staffing copy
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A second White House Situation Room cover transmits the AmeriFlora remarks package, marked for review and distribution.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House Staffing Memorandum for AmeriFlora presidential remarks
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The staffing memorandum routes the April 16 draft of the AmeriFlora presidential remarks for action, concurrence, comment, or information across the White House staff.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Curt Smith memorandum to the President re AmeriFlora 1992, staffing copy
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A staffing-copy memorandum from Curt Smith again briefs the President on the April 20 AmeriFlora remarks, the First Lady introduction, Bob Hope, and the Columbus/discovery message.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Draft Three AmeriFlora presidential remarks, staffing copy
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A second copy of Draft Three of the AmeriFlora remarks prepares the President's Columbus speech on discovery, trade, open cities, open hearts, education, and the spirit of exploration.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Dorrance Smith memorandum re April 11 welfare radio address media usage
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Dorrance Smith reports on radio and television usage of the President's April 11 welfare radio address, including ABC, CBS, NBC/Mutual/Westwood One, UPI, Unistar, CNN, CNBC, and a Washington Post article on welfare responsibility.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Mike Boskin note to the President re March housing starts
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Mike Boskin tells the President that March housing starts rose more than expected and ties the data to employment, income, spending, trade numbers, and possible first-quarter GDP growth.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: CEA memorandum re March housing starts Commerce Department release
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Michael J. Boskin's formal memorandum previews the March housing starts release, noting a fourth consecutive monthly increase, building permits, and a housing starts chart.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Boyden Gray packet re laws Congress should apply to itself
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Boyden Gray responds to the President's handwritten request for bullet points on laws Congress should apply to itself, including Title VII, labor-management relations, health and safety, family and medical leave, independent counsel, deficit acts, Hatch Act, privacy, and FOIA.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Gary Blumenthal and USTR packet re Canadian provincial beer import practices
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Gary Blumenthal sends the President background on the GATT decision over Canadian provincial beer practices, with Julius Katz's USTR memorandum summarizing Canada's plan and U.S. concerns about effective implementation.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Mike Boskin note to the President re February merchandise trade
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Mike Boskin reports that the February merchandise trade deficit was the lowest since 1983, exports reached an all-time high, the stock market hit another high, and unemployment claims continued to fall.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: CEA memorandum re merchandise exports and imports in February
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Michael J. Boskin's formal memorandum previews the February merchandise exports and imports release, describing the trade-deficit decline, export gains, import trends, and a merchandise exports and imports chart.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Telephone memoranda and Signal Switchboard log: April 20, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: White House telephone memoranda record April 20 calls for President Bush, including messages and connected calls through the President's line and the Signal Switchboard.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential Movements: Kennebunkport, Columbus, and Washington, April 20, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The movement logs track the President from Walker's Point to Pease Air National Guard Base, Columbus, Andrews Air Force Base, the White House, the South Grounds, and the Residence.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House Easter Egg Roll family and safety handouts
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Easter Egg Roll packet includes an attached-document marker, bicycle-safety and fire-safety sheets, Barbara Bush's welcome note for A Family Easter at the White House, and an activities map.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Pool Report #8: Kennebunkport to Columbus
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Pool Report #8 covers the President's helicopter, motorcade, and Air Force One trip from Kennebunkport to Columbus for AmeriFlora, including the downtown fly-by and traveling party.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential note to Marlin Fitzwater re family planning and Time article
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President asks Marlin Fitzwater to tell Time that he still favors family planning and that the administration is doing more worldwide than predecessors, with instructions to research his congressional family-planning work and an attached U.S. News article on gender politics and abortion.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Patty Presock photograph packet of Ranger aboard aircraft
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A Patty Presock note says the photographs were for the daily and that copies were sent to Sam and Ellie, followed by color photographs of Ranger aboard an aircraft.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential note to Dick Darman and Boyden Gray with Research Recommendations paperwork article
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President asks Dick Darman and Boyden Gray whether a highlighted Research Recommendations passage about government paperwork costs is accurate, with the April 6 National Institute of Business Management newsletter pages attached.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to Rita Coolidge re Go to Brazil tape
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President tells Rita Coolidge that he watched Olivia Newton-John's Go to Brazil tape, listened to the plea, and will make a decision soon while defending U.S. environmental efforts and budget constraints.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Handwritten note from the President to Meg Louie Bagby re clips
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President writes Mrs. J. Thomas Bagby that he received her April 6 letter, wrote Queida Rauch, and thanks her for sending clips.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to D.C. Talk re Dove Awards
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President congratulates Toby, Kevin, and Michael of D.C. Talk on their recent Dove Awards and sends best wishes from himself and Barbara.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: AmeriFlora photograph packet with President Bush, Barbara Bush, and Bob Hope
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Color photographs show President Bush, Barbara Bush, Bob Hope, and other participants around the AmeriFlora exhibition and related White House settings.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Daily News Clips: April 20, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The daily news-clips packet compiles April 20 coverage and commentary from the Washington Post, Washington Times, Wall Street Journal, USA Today, New York Times, and other outlets on abortion, campaign finance, AmeriFlora, Bush power-walking, campaign strategy, crime, housing, and related political issues.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to Jane Seymour re Go to Brazil tape
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President tells Jane Seymour that he watched Olivia Newton-John's Go to Brazil tape, listened to the plea, and will make a decision soon while discussing U.S. environmental work and financial constraints.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: National Affairs Briefing program brochure, Dallas, August 21-22, 1980
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Roundtable's National Affairs Briefing brochure for Reunion Arena in Dallas includes a statement of purpose, notes pages, program schedules, and speaker profiles for religious and political figures including Charles Stanley, James Robison, Bill Stanmeyer, Ed McAteer, Pat Robertson, Bill Armstrong, Jesse Helms, and W.A. Criswell.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to William S. Broomfield re House retirement decision
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President writes Representative William S. Broomfield that he will miss Broomfield's leadership, decency, honor, counsel, and support, and appends handwritten reflections on the decision.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Recommended telephone call packet to Governor John Ashcroft re Missouri precinct caucuses
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Bush-Quayle campaign recommends that the President call Governor John Ashcroft to congratulate him on the Missouri precinct caucus results; the packet includes a Missouri GOP article and a presidential phone-call note.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary excerpts re Earth Summit and trade talks
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: White House News Summary pages cover President Bush's uncertainty about attending the Earth Summit and international news on tough U.S. trade talks.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Departure statement for Prime Minister Cavaco Silva
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The departure statement for Portuguese Prime Minister Cavaco Silva discusses Iberian discovery, European unity, Yugoslavia, the Uruguay Round, GATT, and U.S.-EC partnership.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House Staffing Memorandum for Washington Redskins presidential remarks
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The staffing memorandum routes the April 22 presidential remarks for the Washington Redskins event, noting that the attached material has been forwarded to the President.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Curt Smith memorandum to the President re Washington Redskins remarks
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Curt Smith briefs the President on proposed remarks for the Washington Redskins event and notes the setting, players, coaches, and themes for honoring the Super Bowl champions.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Draft Three presidential remarks for the Washington Redskins Rose Garden event
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Draft Three of the Redskins remarks welcomes Joe Gibbs, Charley Casserly, players, coaches, and friends while praising the Super Bowl champions and their charitable work.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House Staffing Memorandum for Forum of the Americas presidential remarks
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The staffing memorandum routes the Forum of the Americas remarks for the April 23 Sheraton Washington event.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Speech memorandum to the President re Forum of the Americas remarks
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A speech memorandum forwards proposed Forum of the Americas remarks to the President through David Demarest.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Draft Five presidential remarks for the Forum of the Americas
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Draft Five of the Forum of the Americas remarks discusses the Enterprise for the Americas Initiative, Latin American democracy, free trade, NAFTA, GATT, and open markets.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: This Morning's News: April 22, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The American Political Network briefing covers Pennsylvania, Perot, Clinton, Earth Day, Senate races, House races, TV monitoring, abortion, and campaign coverage for April 22.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Washington Redskins Rose Garden remarks cards
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Large-type remarks cards welcome the 1991-92 Washington Redskins to the White House and praise the team, Joe Gibbs, the defense, and players' charitable work.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: FYI note to the President re David Laughter and Bill Reilly lunch
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A handwritten FYI note tells the President that Jan Naylor called about David Laughter being offered an Agriculture job and notes lunch with Bill Reilly.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: UPI Wire Story: Bush says every day is Earth Day; Brown shows up on the scene
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Thomas Ferraro's UPI story reports on President Bush's Earth Day comments and Jerry Brown's appearance on the scene.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Constance Horner memorandum to the President re remaining ambassadorships
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Constance Horner updates the President on remaining political ambassadorial posts including Saudi Arabia, Croatia, Seychelles, and the Bermuda consulate, with presidential handwritten decisions.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Ede Holiday and USTR packet re Section 337 rotary printing machines order
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Ede Holiday asks the President to decide whether to allow a Section 337 order on certain rotary printing machines to go into effect, attaching USTR Carla Hills's recommendation.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Brent Scowcroft packet for OPIC letters to President Mubarak and President Ben Ali
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Brent Scowcroft asks the President to sign letters to President Hosni Mubarak of Egypt and President Ben Ali of Tunisia for OPIC President Frank Zeder's investment mission.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to Rich Bond re Prescott Bush Dinner
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President sends warm greetings to RNC Chairman Rich Bond and Connecticut Republicans for the 1992 Prescott Bush Dinner honoring Chris Shays and Betty Hudson.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press release recognizing Vicki Davis as the 752nd Daily Point of Light
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Office of the Press Secretary announces Vicki Davis of Little Rock, Arkansas, as the 752nd Daily Point of Light for her volunteer work.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press release on signed legislation re Greenville federal court and humanitarian aid
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Office of the Press Secretary announces the President's signing of H.R. 3686 on Greenville federal court locations and S. 985 on humanitarian aid.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press release announcing Nathaniel M. Gorton judicial nomination
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Office of the Press Secretary announces the President's intention to nominate Nathaniel M. Gorton as United States District Judge for Massachusetts.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press release on signing S. 1743, Arkansas Wild and Scenic Rivers Act of 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Office of the Press Secretary announces the President's signing of S. 1743 designating segments of eight Arkansas rivers as part of the National Wild and Scenic Rivers System.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Duplicate press release copies announcing James D. Jameson Commerce nomination
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Duplicate Office of the Press Secretary copies announce the President's intention to nominate James D. Jameson as Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Trade Development.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Statement by the President on Earth Day
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President's Earth Day statement calls for everyday environmental action and urges Congress to pass energy, wetlands, safe drinking water, pesticide, recycling, and wastewater legislation.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press release: remarks by the President and Prime Minister Cavaco Silva upon departure
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Office of the Press Secretary releases the President's and Prime Minister Cavaco Silva's South Lawn departure remarks on European unity, Yugoslavia, and transatlantic partnership.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press briefing by Assistant Secretary Thomas Niles re European and Canadian affairs
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Assistant Secretary Thomas Niles briefs the press after the U.S.-EC meeting on trade, agriculture, GATT, Yugoslavia, environmental questions, and European relations.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Materials Forwarded to the President: April 22, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The forwarding list inventories action items, classified material, information memos, remarks, and schedule materials sent to the President on April 22.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Rose Garden Ceremony and Barbecue for the Redskins guest list and regrets
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The guest list for the Rose Garden Ceremony and Barbecue for the Redskins lists the President and Mrs. Bush, players, coaches, staff, guests, and regrets.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Fred Malek political and fundraising plan packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Fred Malek sends Sam Skinner a political and fundraising plan outlining Bush-Quayle funding needs, confirmed finance events, Victory '92, candidate events, compliance costs, and voter-contact activities.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter to Harold Solomon and Dreamers photograph packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President declines Harold Solomon's tennis invitation because of a short Florida visit; attached photographs carry presidential notes to Jamie Bush and the Dreamers.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Jasper Textiles and Outer Banks sports-shirt gift packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes the President's handwritten thank-you note to William Hunter Jr. for Outer Banks sports shirts, a Jasper Textiles letter, packing slip, and photocopied shirt image.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Second copy of Rose Garden Ceremony and Barbecue for the Redskins guest list
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A second copy of the Redskins Rose Garden guest list repeats the President and Mrs. Bush, team members, coaches, staff, and guests.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Signing of U.S.-Japan paper trade agreement remarks cards
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Remarks cards for the Roosevelt Room signing of the U.S.-Japan paper trade agreement emphasize market access, equal partnership, and open trade.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Burton J. Lee letter to the President re Manfred Werner's case
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Burton J. Lee updates the President on Manfred Werner's colon cancer surgery, pathology questions, and treatment considerations, with a handwritten presidential note.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Telephone memoranda and Signal Switchboard log: April 22, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Telephone memorandum pages and a Signal Switchboard sheet record April 22 call entries and contacts for President Bush.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential Movements: Washington, D.C., April 22, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The presidential movement log records the President's Washington, D.C. movements on April 22, 1992, beginning with the doctor's office and White House entries.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential phone-call note re MFB and Treasury restoration
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A handwritten Presidential Phone Calls form records an April 22 call notation involving MFB, Treasury restoration, and follow-up by Cathy to call Hollis and set it up.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Sandy Muir correspondence and Bush philosophy commentary packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks Sandy Muir for writing and sending an editorial; the packet includes a routing note, Muir's April 17 letter, and the commentary article 'Define the Man to See the President.'

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Jonathan Bush and Jerry Cammarata resume packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President tells Jonathan Bush he is passing Jerry Cammarata's resume to Connie Horner; the packet includes Jonathan Bush's letter, Cammarata's letter, resume, publications, and education pages.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: State Department and Protocol fax packet re Portugal/EC official working visit
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The State Department and Office of Protocol transmit material on the Portugal/EC official working visit, including schedule, briefing, and room information.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press release: remarks by the President to the Washington Redskins
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Office of the Press Secretary releases the President's Rose Garden remarks welcoming the 1992 Super Bowl champion Washington Redskins.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Second copy of presidential note to William Hunter Jr. re Jasper Textiles shirts
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A second copy of the President's handwritten thank-you note to William Hunter Jr. thanks him for the Outer Banks sports shirts, with highlighted bcc notation to Nicholas Brady.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Willard Johnson correspondence and clippings reply packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks Willard Johnson for a heartfelt letter and clippings, says he will share the observations with staff, and the packet includes Johnson's March 23 letter.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Alexander Graham Bell Association honorary chairmen scheduling packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet covers President and Mrs. Bush accepting honorary chairmanship of the Alexander Graham Bell Association for the Deaf benefit, including letter drafts and copies, invitation material, counsel review, Susan Porter Rose and Patricia Cox routing, and scheduling recommendation forms.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Barry Williamson Texas drop-by and state fundraiser packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President replies to Barry Williamson about the possibility of a drop-by in Texas; attached Williamson, Bob Teeter, and Bobby Burchfield material discusses a state-candidate fundraiser and related campaign ramifications.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Handwritten letter to Richard Jackman and Dick Jackman note packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President writes Richard Jackman from aboard Air Force One about the campaign, the economy, Clinton, and Ross Perot; attached material includes Jackman's note about Dick Cheney at Valley Forge Military Academy and campaign-style talking points.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press release: commencement address to Florida International University
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Office of the Press Secretary release transmits President Bush's Florida International University commencement remarks at the Miami Beach Convention Center, covering service, freedom, education, and Michael Yelovich.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: George W. Bush, Prescott Bush, and Beth Bush visit schedules
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Schedule pages list George W. Bush's April 27 and April 29 travel and Prescott and Beth Bush's April 28-29 Washington visit plans.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Muriel Siebert, Barbara Bush, and economic advice correspondence packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes a George Bush reply to Muriel Siebert, presidential and staff notes to Phil Brady and Mrs. Bush, Siebert's cover note, and her letter to Barbara Bush on women in the Republican Party, bank capital rules, and economic conditions.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Second copy of Prescott and Beth Bush Washington visit schedule
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A second copy of the April 27 memorandum lists Prescott and Beth Bush's April 28-29 Washington visit schedule.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to Heinz C. Prechter re Vietnam trade embargo
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks Heinz Prechter for suggestions on the Vietnam trade embargo, agrees on Southeast Asian market opportunities, and explains POW/MIA leverage and limits on easing the embargo.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Handwritten note from the President to Robert W. Blake re poll, Perot, and Florida
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks Bob Blake for a poll, predicts Perot will eventually self-destruct, and notes he is rushing off to Florida.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Handwritten note from the President to Bill Gilbert re They Also Served
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President tells Bill Gilbert that Fred Valentine and Carl Warwick brought him They Also Served and that he looks forward to reading it.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Handwritten note from the President to Andre W. Brewster re Caves Valley outing
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks Andre Brewster for Saturday's hospitality, the Caves Valley outing, and the club and golf course.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Second copy of handwritten letter to Richard Jackman re campaign and Perot
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A second copy of the President's Aboard Air Force One handwritten letter to Richard Jackman discusses ugly press coverage, the economy, Clinton, and Ross Perot.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Caves Valley Club thank-you notes to Leslie Disharoon, Dennis Satyshur, and Abe Resnick
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes Caves Valley Club thank-you notes and address material for Leslie Disharoon, golf pro Dennis Satyshur, and Commissioner Abe Resnick after the President's Saturday outing.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Daily News Clips: April 27, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The daily news-clips packet compiles April 27 coverage and commentary from the Washington Post, Washington Times, Wall Street Journal, USA Today, and New York Times on Clinton, Perot, congressional scandals, the Earth Summit, crime, former Soviet republics, Afghanistan, abortion, campaign finance, and related political issues.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Florida International University commencement and April 27 trip photograph packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Color photographs and a White House photo-stamp page show President Bush at Florida International University commencement and related April 27 travel or event settings.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Fact sheet on capital market regulation reforms
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Office of the Press Secretary fact sheet describes SEC and CFTC regulatory changes on capital market regulation, asset-backed financing, Rule 144A, commodity pools, and small-business disclosure.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Text of letter to Armed Services Committee chairmen re National Defense Authorization Act report
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Office of the Press Secretary releases the text of the President's April 28 letter to the Senate and House Armed Services Committee chairmen transmitting a report pursuant to the National Defense Authorization Act.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Statement by the President on signing H.J. Res. 402 re George Mason memorial
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President states that he has signed H.J. Res. 402 approving the location of a George Mason memorial in the District of Columbia and its environs.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Statement by the Press Secretary on the President's meeting with Yegor Gaydar
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The statement reports the President's Oval Office meeting with Russian First Deputy Prime Minister Yegor Gaydar, focused mainly on Russian economic reform and IMF-related issues.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Message to Congress transmitting the National Endowment for the Humanities fiscal 1991 annual report
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President transmits the 25th Annual Report of the National Endowment for the Humanities for fiscal year 1991 to Congress.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Message to Congress transmitting the Federal Council on the Aging 1991 annual report
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President transmits the Annual Report for 1991 of the Federal Council on the Aging under the Older Americans Act.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Message to Congress transmitting the Job Training 2000 Act
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President transmits proposed Job Training 2000 Act legislation to reform the federal vocational training system through local skill centers, labor-market information, and related services.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Congressional Monitor and Congress In Print packet: April 28, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes the April 28 Congressional Monitor issue, congressional schedules and committee listings, CQ legislative advertising material, and a Congress In Print issue.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Statement by the Press Secretary on the President's meeting with President Turgut Ozal
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The statement reports the President's Oval Office meeting with Turkish President Turgut Ozal and notes discussions of Cyprus, Iraq, Central Asia, and Bosnia.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press release: remarks by the President at Bush/Quayle campaign headquarters
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Office of the Press Secretary releases the President's remarks at Bush/Quayle campaign headquarters in Washington, D.C., including comments on campaign strategy, education, and congressional change.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Loyalty Day, 1992 proclamation
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The proclamation designates May 1, 1992, as Loyalty Day and calls on Americans and government officials to observe the day with appropriate ceremonies and flag displays.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Telephone memoranda for President Bush: April 28, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Telephone memorandum pages for President Bush and the Signal Switchboard list April 28 call activity, including a morning outgoing call to Katherine Super and a no-calls page.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential movements log: Washington, D.C., April 28, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The WHCA presidential movements log records President Bush's April 28 movements in Washington, D.C., from the South Grounds to the Oval Office, Cabinet Room, Rose Garden, and Residence.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press release: remarks by the President at the President's Dinner
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Office of the Press Secretary releases the President's evening remarks at the President's Dinner at the Washington Convention Center, including comments on Republican leadership and the Democratic Congress.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Stephen P. Farrar resignation and presidential acceptance letter packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President accepts Stephen P. Farrar's resignation as Special Assistant to the President for Policy Development as Farrar moves to become chief of staff at the Office of the U.S. Trade Representative; the packet includes Farrar's April 9 resignation letter.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Edgar M. Bronfman World Jewish Congress invitation reply packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President declines Edgar M. Bronfman's invitation to address the World Jewish Congress conference in Brussels because it conflicts with the Economic Summit; the packet includes outgoing copy, signed copy, and Bronfman's April 1 invitation.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Nicholas E. Calio weekly legislative report: April 28-May 1, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Nicholas E. Calio's weekly report to the President covers campaign finance reform, NASA authorization, family planning, rescissions, next week's congressional agenda, and selected congressional activity.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Memorandum and handwritten notes re Expo '92 U.S. National Day performer
