directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A memorandum to the President discusses the search for a performer for National Day of the United States at Expo '92 after Whitney Houston backed out, with related handwritten notes and the President's handwritten comment that he cannot get into the matter.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Daily News Clips: April 28, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The daily news-clips packet compiles April 28 coverage and commentary from the Washington Post, Washington Times, Wall Street Journal, USA Today, and New York Times on the House Bank, Ross Perot, Clinton, Russia and the IMF, Syria, Iraq, the President's Dinner, campaign finance, food guidelines, and related political issues.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to David Cousens and Patten White re Maine lobster fishermen
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President tells David Cousens and Patten White of the Maine Lobstermen's Association that he has passed along their concerns while weighing conservation issues and jobs for Maine lobster fishermen and workers.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to Alison Gregg Corcoran re birth greeting
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President and Mrs. Bush welcome Alison Gregg Corcoran after her April 4 birth and send wishes for health and happiness.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to William Conor re birth greeting
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President and Mrs. Bush welcome William Conor after his February 27 birth and send wishes for health and happiness.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to Christopher M. Blessington re Dallas event advance and Secret Service staffs
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks Christopher M. Blessington for recognizing the Advance and Secret Service staffs assigned to a Dallas event and for his own work on the event.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to Captain Craig A. Pendleton re Maine fishing levels
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President tells Captain Craig A. Pendleton of the Maine Fisherman's Cooperative Association that staff experts will review materials on proposed reductions in fishing levels.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to N. J. Nicholas Jr. re trade and environmental resignations
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President accepts N. J. Nicholas Jr.'s resignations from the Advisory Committee on Trade Policy and Negotiations and the President's Commission on Environmental Quality.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to Archbishop Iakovos re Easter message and American conscience
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks Archbishop Iakovos for Easter prayers and reflections on a reawakening of the American conscience, replying with comments on optimism, national purpose, and newly democratic nations.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to the Reverend William J. Byron re Catholic University retirement
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President congratulates the Reverend William J. Byron, S.J., on his retirement as President of The Catholic University of America.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to James L. Morrow re Navy service offer
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks James L. Morrow for his patriotism, high regard for the Navy, and generous offer of services in connection with the situation regarding Iraq.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Second copy of letter from the President to Edgar M. Bronfman re World Jewish Congress invitation
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A second copy of the President's letter to Edgar M. Bronfman declines an invitation to address the World Jewish Congress in Brussels because of an Economic Summit scheduling conflict.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to Emile Roy re 72nd birthday
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President and Mrs. Bush send Emile Roy best wishes for his 72nd birthday, with address and Jan Burmeister routing material on the page.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Bush/Quayle campaign headquarters photograph packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Color photographs show President Bush speaking at Bush/Quayle campaign headquarters, with a sticky-note label identifying campaign headquarters in Washington, D.C.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: National Science Bowl winners photograph
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A color photograph shows President Bush with a group identified by sticky note as National Science Bowl winners.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Big Brother Big Sister photograph
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A color photograph shows President Bush and Mrs. Bush with guests identified by sticky note as Big Brother Big Sister.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Ozal of Turkey meeting photograph packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Color photographs show President Bush with Turkish President Turgut Ozal and meeting participants in White House settings, including a sticky-note label reading President Ozal of Turkey.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House staffing memorandum re presidential remarks for regulation reform ceremony
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A White House staffing memorandum routes presidential remarks for the April 29 regulation reform ceremony, with action and FYI checkmarks for senior staff.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Joseph P. Duggan memorandum to the President re regulation reform ceremony
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Joseph P. Duggan briefs the President on the April 29 Rose Garden regulation reform ceremony and the 90-day regulatory review initiative.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Draft Five presidential remarks for regulation reform ceremony
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Duggan/Simon Draft Five remarks for the Rose Garden regulation reform ceremony discuss limited government, social engineering, regulatory costs, market-based environmental policy, and extension of the regulatory moratorium.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House staffing memorandum re von Weizsaecker arrival ceremony and dinner toast remarks
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A White House staffing memorandum routes presidential remarks for the arrival ceremony and dinner toast during the visit of German President Richard von Weizsaecker.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Beth Hinchliffe memorandum to the President re von Weizsaecker welcome and toast remarks
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Beth Hinchliffe briefs the President on welcoming German President von Weizsaecker at the South Lawn ceremony and delivering the state dinner toast.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Draft Two presidential welcome remarks for German President von Weizsaecker
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Draft Two welcome remarks for the South Lawn arrival ceremony discuss German democracy, partnership in leadership, Europe after the Cold War, and von Weizsaecker's moral leadership.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Draft Two presidential state dinner toast for German President von Weizsaecker
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Draft Two of the state dinner toast honors President von Weizsaecker, German unity, German-American partnership, and the Washington salute to German culture.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Final presidential remarks cards for regulation reform ceremony
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Final-card text for the April 29 Rose Garden regulation reform ceremony salutes regulatory reform leaders, attacks social engineering and hidden regulatory costs, extends the moratorium, and announces a veto threat against excessive new regulatory burdens.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Protocol fax and gift profile for President von Weizsaecker's gift
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Office of Protocol fax and attached profile describe President von Weizsaecker's gift, a hand-crafted model of the frigate Berlin.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Roone Arledge telephone follow-up packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A phone-call follow-up packet notes that Roone Arledge of ABC News was hospitalized after prostate cancer surgery, gives a Johns Hopkins number, and includes a contact-record page.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Final presidential state dinner toast cards for President von Weizsaecker
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Final-card text for the state dinner toast welcomes President and Mrs. von Weizsaecker, recalls German unification and the Liberty Bell in Berlin, and celebrates German-American partnership.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: RZ note to the President re Laurie Firestone seating charts and Nick Brady meeting
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A note to the President says Laurie Firestone needs time to show seating charts and Nick Brady wants five minutes to discuss the Federal Reserve and money growth.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: RZ note to the President re Scowcroft and Yeutter meeting before going home
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A note to the President states that General Brent Scowcroft and Clayton Yeutter would like to see him before he goes home that night.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Dr. Burton J. Lee memorandum to the President re General Woerner
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Dr. Burton J. Lee tells the President he reviewed the final documents on General Woerner, discussed Woerner's chemotherapy treatment by telephone, and regards the prognosis as guarded over one year.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential phone-call note page: April 29, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A handwritten presidential phone-call note page lists April 29 call items, including the Kennedy Association, DOD versus CIA, Toy McLeary, and a call about 'family'.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary excerpts: April 29, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: White House News Summary pages cover Rep. Michel and the House Bank subpoena, financial-regulation changes, and Pennsylvania primary results.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Patty Bush White House visit and transportation note
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A schedule note records Patty Bush's April 29-May 1 Washington visit, White House tour group, state dinner attendance, and requested White House car arrangements.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Mrs. Bush's daily press clippings: April 29, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Mrs. Bush's press-clippings packet includes AP wires on commemorative coins, the President's Dinner fundraiser, and Michael Kojima, plus Washington Post and AP clippings on White House menus, reading, and Hillary Clinton.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Material forwarded to the President: April 29, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A two-page checklist lists action items, classified responses, remarks, and schedule materials forwarded to the President, including gift letters, regulatory reform materials, and von Weizsaecker event cards.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Sequence of events for official dinner honoring President von Weizsaecker
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The official dinner sequence lays out the President and Mrs. Bush's North Portico greeting, receiving line, state dinner toasts, dinner, coffee, and after-dinner guest greeting.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Guest list for state dinner honoring President von Weizsaecker
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The state dinner guest list includes President and Mrs. von Weizsaecker, German officials, U.S. officials, business leaders, cultural figures, and other guests.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Menu and program for state dinner honoring President von Weizsaecker
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The state dinner menu and program include the dinner menu, cover pages, and entertainment program featuring Barbara Cook and Wally Harper.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Entertainment card for Barbara Cook and Wally Harper at von Weizsaecker state dinner
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The entertainment card gives presidential talking points thanking Barbara Cook and Wally Harper for the state dinner performance.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Schedule of the President: April 29, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President's April 29 schedule lists staff time, the Taft Institute photo, briefings, the von Weizsaecker arrival ceremony, political and campaign meetings, regulatory reform remarks, and the state dinner.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Photo opportunity briefing with Robert A. Taft Institute board members
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The briefing describes the April 29 Rose Garden Steps photo opportunity with Robert A. Taft Institute board members and lists participants.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Sequence of events for von Weizsaecker arrival ceremony, reception, meeting, and coffee
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The sequence covers the April 29 South Lawn arrival ceremony for President and Mrs. von Weizsaecker, Blue Room reception, Oval Office and Cabinet Room meetings, and Mrs. Bush's coffee.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Political speech meeting briefing with the President
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Samuel K. Skinner's briefing page sets out the April 29 Oval Office political speech meeting with Peggy Noonan and Bob Teeter.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Campaign meeting briefing with the President
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Samuel K. Skinner's briefing page sets out the April 29 Roosevelt Room campaign meeting to discuss the status of campaign activities.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Regulatory reform 90-day moratorium speech briefing
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The briefing prepares the President for the April 29 Rose Garden speech marking the conclusion of the 90-day regulatory moratorium and outlining continuing regulatory reform steps.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Second copy of sequence of events for official dinner honoring President von Weizsaecker
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A second copy of the official dinner sequence repeats the North Portico greeting, receiving line, state dinner toasts, dinner, coffee, and after-dinner greeting plan.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Ronald Reagan correspondence and phone note re Soviet documents declassification
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes the President's reply to Ronald Reagan on May 7 travel, Reagan Library declassification of former Soviet Union records, and family matters, Reagan's April 24 letter, and a presidential phone-call note.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Fact sheet on the President's regulatory reform initiative
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Office of the Press Secretary fact sheet summarizes the President's regulatory reform initiative, extension of the moratorium, cost-benefit analysis, FDA reforms, worker rights, financing costs, transportation costs, environmental goals, and export barriers.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Estimated annual cost savings table from the 90-day regulatory reform initiative
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A table estimates annual cost savings by department and agency from actions announced during the 90-day regulatory reform initiative.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Chronology of the President's regulatory reform initiative as of April 28, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The chronology lists regulatory reform initiative actions from the January 28 moratorium announcement through late-April agency actions.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Highlights of the President's regulatory reform initiative
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The highlights sheet summarizes projected savings, FDA reforms, worker-rights notices, credit-cost reductions, environmental flexibility, export regulation changes, and the 120-day extension.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Proclamation to amend the Generalized System of Preferences
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President issues a proclamation amending the Generalized System of Preferences under the Trade Act of 1974.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential memorandum for heads of departments and agencies re benefits and costs of legislative proposals
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President directs departments and agencies to establish procedures disclosing likely benefits and costs of legislative proposals to the public and Congress before enactment.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press briefing by Boyden Gray and Michael Boskin on regulatory reform
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Press briefing transcript with Counsel to the President Boyden Gray and CEA Chairman Michael Boskin covers regulatory reform, cost savings, the moratorium, cost-benefit analysis, OSHA, FDA, and environmental regulation.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press release: remarks by the President at arrival ceremony for President von Weizsaecker
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Office of the Press Secretary releases the President's South Grounds arrival-ceremony remarks welcoming German President von Weizsaecker.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential memorandum for certain department and agency heads re implementing regulatory reforms
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President's memorandum to certain department and agency heads extends implementation of regulatory reforms and directs continued adherence to the regulatory moratorium and substantive standards.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Chris Evert and Andy Mill visit schedule and attachment note
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A Patty Presock note and memorandum for the President and Mrs. Bush lay out Chris Evert and Andy Mill's April 29-May 3 White House visit schedule with their son Alex and nanny.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Recommended telephone call to Ronald B. Leighton re Western District of Washington judgeship
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A recommended telephone call sheet asks the President to call Ronald B. Leighton to ask him to serve as U.S. District Judge for the Western District of Washington.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: AP wire on Rodney King verdict with handwritten note to the President
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: An AP wire reports the acquittal of four Los Angeles police officers in the Rodney King beating case; a handwritten note warns the President he may get questions after dinner at the mix-and-mingle.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: John Riggins Football Hall of Fame presenter request packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A packet includes the outgoing response to John Riggins, a note to Kathy Super, Alan Simpson's forwarding letter, and Riggins's request that President Bush present him at the Pro Football Hall of Fame induction.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Congressional Monitor: Thursday, May 7, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Congressional Quarterly's Congressional Monitor covers the Senate and House schedule, floor action, committee work, appropriations status, and congressional issues for May 7.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House staffing memorandum re presidential remarks at Mount Zion Missionary Baptist Church
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A White House staffing memorandum routes presidential remarks for the May 7 National Day of Prayer event at Mount Zion Missionary Baptist Church in Los Angeles.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Jennifer Grossman memorandum to the President re Mount Zion Missionary Baptist Church
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Jennifer Grossman briefs the President for the National Day of Prayer appearance at Mount Zion Missionary Baptist Church following the Los Angeles riots.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Draft One presidential remarks for Mount Zion Missionary Baptist Church
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Draft One remarks for Mount Zion Church address prayer, rebuilding Los Angeles, law and justice, and the meaning of community after the riots.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary: May 7, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House News Summary covers national, international, network, and political news for the morning of May 7.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: The Daily Briefing on American Politics: May 7, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The APN/Hotline daily briefing summarizes campaign, polling, party, congressional, gubernatorial, and media developments for May 7.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President's unpublished schedule: May 7, 1992

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Mount Zion Baptist Church National Day of Prayer service briefing
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The briefing prepares the President for the National Day of Prayer service at Mount Zion Baptist Church, including purpose, background, participants, remarks, and press plan.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Meeting briefing with neighborhood community leaders
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The briefing lays out the President's May 7 meeting with Los Angeles neighborhood community leaders, including purpose, background, participants, press plan, and a participant list.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Talking points for meeting with neighborhood community leaders

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Meeting briefing and talking points with elected officials from the Los Angeles area
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The briefing packet prepares the President for a meeting with Los Angeles-area elected officials and includes participants, press plan, talking points, and a seating chart.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Meeting briefing and talking points with members of the Korean-American community
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The briefing packet prepares the President for a Los Angeles meeting with Korean-American community leaders and includes participants, press plan, background, and talking points.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Radio Korea address briefing
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The briefing prepares the President to address the Korean-American community over the Radio Korea network.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Meeting briefing and talking points on Hispanic economic development
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The briefing packet prepares the President for a meeting with Hispanic leaders on economic development in Los Angeles, including background, participants, press plan, and talking points.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Mrs. Bush's daily press clippings: May 7, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Mrs. Bush's Press Office clipping packet includes Doro Bush wedding coverage, White House and presidential-pet features, and related society and press clippings.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Telephone memoranda: May 7, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Telephone memorandum pages record no presidential calls and Signal Switchboard calls involving Mrs. Barbara P. Bush on May 7.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential movements: Los Angeles, May 7, 1992

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Chicago Sun-Times clipping: Minority staff explains rap, racism to Bush

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Ross Perot press clipping packet

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Tax Time '92 signed photograph to Terri

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush telephone memoranda for May 22, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The telephone memoranda list May 22 calls routed through the Signal Switchboard, including Nicholas Calio, Digger Phelps, Rose Zamaria, Richard Darman, Dorothy LeBlond, Jonathan Bush, Marvin Bush, and others.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Mrs. Bush's Press Office daily press clippings, May 22, 1992

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential Movements: Kennebunkport, Maine, May 22, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The movements log records President Bush departing Walker's Point by boat, returning to Walker's Point, visiting Cape Arundel Golf Course, and returning by motorcade.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: David L. Brennan budget-cap advice packet with Notre Dame speech enclosure
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President replies to David Brennan's advice about spending caps and encloses the Notre Dame speech, which is included as the attached sheet referenced in the reply.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Stuart W. Martin and Lou Bush campaign-volunteer correspondence packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet follows up on Lou Bush's message that Stuart W. Martin wanted to help the campaign, with President-to-Ron Kaufman routing, Kaufman memoranda, letters to Martin, and a Bush-Quayle '92 response.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: California State Firefighters Association correspondence packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks California State Firefighters Association leaders for their May 13 letter and support, with the incoming association letter attached.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Notice to the Press: Kennebunkport, May 22, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Office of the Press Secretary notice lays out the Kennebunkport press schedule for Friday, May 22, including photo opportunities and press-pool movements.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary: 12:00 P.M. News Update, May 22, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The noon news summary covers Yugoslavia, military site closings, Peru, economic and political items, and press coverage relevant to the administration.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Second copy of Pool Report #2: Bush plays golf, slowly
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A second copy repeats Pool Report #2 on President Bush playing golf slowly at Cape Arundel.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Sea Island Jones family photograph inscription correspondence packet

directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Cardinal Bernard Law urges the President to act quickly on Haitians at sea and to expand in-country refugee processing in Port-au-Prince, with a Cardinal's Residence fax cover sheet.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Argentina America '92 exposition memorandum and draft presidential reply to Carlos Menem

directScanItemizedRecords.title: George W. Bush memorandum on Balanced Budget Amendment momentum
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: George W. Bush sends the President, Skinner, Teeter, and Malek an action plan for getting ahead of the Balanced Budget Amendment issue.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Leonard Garment and Jacques Torczyner Middle East policy correspondence packet

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Friday Follies political cartoon packet, May 22, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Friday Follies packet collects political cartoons and cartoon clippings on Quayle, Clinton, Perot, urban policy, the Rio conference, Gorbachev, Congress, and the 1992 campaign.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Congressional Monitor issue, May 22, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Congressional Monitor issue covers news from the Hill, committee and floor schedules, campaign events, fiscal 1993 appropriations status, House and Senate floor action, and future listings.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Second copy of Alex Machaskee, Mayor Michael White, and Cleveland Trip News packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A second copy repeats the Alex Machaskee letter, attached Mayor Michael White letter, and Trip News clipping on Bush's Cleveland family-values appearance.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: The Hotline issue, May 22, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Hotline issue surveys White House '92, Issues '92, Senate Watch, House race briefings, redistricting, hotspots, California cable, poll updates, and morning television coverage.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Bob Elliott Portland horseshoe tournament memorandum and Channel 6 fax

directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A second copy of the President's May 22 letter reassures Michael Eisner about political preferences and thanks him for supporting Points of Light.

directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes the President's May 31 note to Dick Darman, Jack Kemp, and Lamar Alexander about Cities in Schools funding, William Milliken's note and May 13 letter to Kemp, and Barbara Franklin's later Commerce Department follow-up.

directScanItemizedRecords.type: President's Note and Phone Call Packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President relays John Engler's suggestion that DOD accounting centers be placed in urban centers as an urban strategy and jobs measure, with follow-up copies to senior officials and related phone-call forms.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Mrs. Truly letter query and Camp David phone-call note
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President asks Rose Zamaria where the letter from Mrs. Truly is, followed by a Camp David presidential phone-call form with handwritten notes.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: John Engler campaign-anxiety memorandum and phone-call note
directScanItemizedRecords.type: Campaign Strategy Memorandum and Call Note
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President briefs Sam Skinner and Bob Teeter on John Engler's worries about Michigan, Perot, discontent, the North Dallas fund-raiser, and press stories about campaign disarray.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Clinton literacy charge rebuttal packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President sends Bob Teeter material rebutting Bill Clinton's charge that the administration had reduced literacy support, with OMB figures, education statistics, and Lamar Alexander's statement.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Nicholas Brady campaign strategy memorandum packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks Nicholas Brady for a campaign and economy memorandum addressing Perot, Brown, Buchanan voters, discontent, values, and economic framing, with a David Broder column attached.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Iraq policy Boston Globe editorial packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President sends Brent Scowcroft, Dorrance Smith, and Tony Snow a Boston Sunday Globe editorial he calls good, with a headline he likes and a piece he judges acceptable.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Camp David handwritten presidential phone-call notes
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Three Camp David Presidential Phone Calls forms preserve the President's handwritten notes on separate calls and follow-up matters.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Cleveland and inner-cities speech outline packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet contains Bush's handwritten Cleveland outline and typed inner-city speech material built around a six-point plan covering Weed and Seed, HOPE, welfare reform, jobs, job training, education reform, and neighborhood revival.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: William H. Hudnut, Beppie Huidekoper, and Voices article packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President replies to William H. Hudnut, asks about Beppie Huidekoper and Jane's whereabouts, and reviews an attached Voices article or interview excerpt.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Second copy of Iraq policy Boston Globe editorial packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A second copy repeats the President's note to Brent Scowcroft, Dorrance Smith, and Tony Snow and the Boston Globe Iraq policy editorial.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Camp David and Air Force One phone-call note packet

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Curtis Hamilton books correspondence
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks Curtis Hamilton for books sent to him and Mrs. Bush and jokes that Millie may go into space after reading the booklet.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Bradford M. Freeman tennis and golf-driver correspondence
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President replies to Bradford Freeman about tennis, an ugly political climate, and the new golf driver Freeman enclosed after their match.

directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The clipping packet collects press commentary on Republican women, backlash politics, Naomi Wolf, Lynn Yeakel, and women in public life.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Education, Christopher Whittle, and Edison Project clipping packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet gathers education clippings on Christopher Whittle, Benno Schmidt, the Edison Project, reading lists, Channel One, America 2000, and efforts to reinvent education.

directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A second copy repeats the President's Cities in Schools funding note, William Milliken's May 13 letter to Jack Kemp, and Milliken's personal note to the President.

directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A second copy repeats the President's May 31 reply to Bradford Freeman thanking him for the new driver and reflecting on tennis and politics.

directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Michael Boskin briefs the President on April personal income and outlays, consumer spending, real disposable income, and the forthcoming Purchasing Managers' Index release.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Phil Gagle and Doug Wead campaign-support correspondence packet

directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Hotline issue surveys the California primary, Perot, Clinton, Bush, White House operation stories, Iraq, ambassadors, the Year of the Woman, California Senate races, poll updates, and television monitoring.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: A Charter for American-Russian Partnership and Friendship

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Joint U.S.-Russian Statement on a Global Protection System

directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The release describes the bilateral investment treaty signed by the United States and the Russian Federation to protect and encourage investment.

directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The fact sheet describes the new U.S.-Russian agreement on scientific and technical cooperation in fuels and energy.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Open Lands Memorandum of Understanding between the United States and Russia
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The release describes the Memorandum of Understanding on Open Lands signed by the United States and Russia.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: OPIC Investment Incentive Agreement between the United States and the Russian Federation
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The release describes the OPIC investment incentive agreement between the United States and the Russian Federation.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press Release: Opening new U.S. and Russian consulates
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The release announces agreement during the Bush-Yeltsin meetings to open new U.S. and Russian consulates.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Fact Sheet on Removal of Ceilings on U.S. and Russian Personnel

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Joint Statement on Bosnia-Hercegovina

directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The fact sheet summarizes chemical-weapons transparency, destruction, nonproliferation, and confidence-building issues.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Agreement on destruction and safeguarding of weapons and prevention of weapons proliferation
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The release describes an agreement between the United States and Russia on destroying and safeguarding weapons and preventing weapons proliferation.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: U.S.-Russian Civil Aviation Memorandum of Understanding

directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The attachment packet includes the Chuck Norris address page, Kick Drugs Out of America promotional material, and a marked working copy of the President's reply.

directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Burton J. Lee writes Charles E. Wood about medical consultation after Wood's letter to the President, with oncology letterhead and Wood's incoming letter attached.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Wirt A. Yerger Jr. correspondence re courage and foresight

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from President Bush to John Major re Camp David visit and thornproof jacket
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush writes John Major that he and Barbara enjoyed their time at Camp David and thanks him for a thornproof jacket.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Larry Gatlin correspondence packet

directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A Pike County Coal fax from Velma Childers to Rose Zamaria forwards Kentucky political clippings on Ellen Williams, the Ford race, and Republican organizing.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary, June 30, 1992, 6:00 A.M. EDT edition

directScanItemizedRecords.title: The Daily Briefing on American Politics: Tuesday, June 30, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The American Political Network daily briefing tracks the abortion decision as a campaign issue, Perot's ABC appearances and ballot access, Clinton vice-presidential speculation, Bush and Perot, Senate and House races, governors, polling, and television monitoring.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: New York Times excerpts of interview with President Bush on his first term

directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Robert Teeter and Bush-Quayle '92 materials brief President Bush on Floyd Brown, Citizens for Bush, the Presidential Victory Committee, and correspondence with CBS News about campaign activity and contributor communications.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Robert Teeter memo to President Bush re note and conversation with Jerry Falwell
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Robert Teeter responds to President Bush's note and conversation with Jerry Falwell in a campaign memorandum sent to the President.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: D. Allan Bromley memo to President Bush re science and technology report
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: D. Allan Bromley sends President Bush his science and technology report and related material, highlighting accomplishments in science, technology, and OSTP communication.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush handwritten Don Kendall note and campaign-finance article packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush forwards a Don Kendall item with a tongue-in-cheek note, followed by AP and campaign-finance article material concerning Bush campaign strategy and corporate contributors.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Ray Price strategy memo to President Bush: Ruminations #1
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush forwards Ray Price's twelve-page Ruminations #1 strategy memorandum with handwritten routing notes, asking that Jim receive it and marking it for close handling.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Ron Kaufman FYI cover sheet to Rose Zamaria
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Ron Kaufman sends Rose Zamaria an FYI cover sheet on July 17, 1992.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush congratulatory letter to Mrs. Jean Pipes
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush sends Mrs. Jean Pipes a congratulatory letter connected to the Palm Beach County Political Action Committee.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush note on new computer stationery
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A short note to the President presents his new computer stationery and bears President Bush handling marks.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Susan Petersen Kendall Ranch correspondence packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A Susan Petersen correspondence packet includes a From the desk of George Bush routing page, address page for Ms. Susan Petersen at Kendall Ranch in Cora, Wyoming, and handwritten incoming correspondence.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush phone-call note re John Sununu and the Wyoming turn
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A presidential phone-call note records President Bush handling for a John Sununu-related call and a Wyoming turn reference.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush handwritten routing note re Eli, Tahoe, and schedule handling
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A rotated From the desk of George Bush routing note records presidential handwriting about Eli, Tahoe, and schedule handling.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Telephone memoranda for President Bush, July 17, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A no-handwritten-notes marker and White House telephone memorandum pages record July 17 call handling for President Bush, Signal Switchboard entries, and calls involving senior officials and Premier John Swan.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential movements log: Boulder, Jackson, and Salt Lake City, July 17, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The WHCA movements log records President Bush's July 17 movements from Boulder, Wyoming, through Jackson and Salt Lake City.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Utah Bush-Quayle Leadership Meeting participant list
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet lists participants for the Utah Bush-Quayle Leadership Meeting, providing names and affiliations for the Salt Lake City event.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Meeting with LDS Church leaders agenda and talking-point cards
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The briefing cards prepare President Bush for a meeting with LDS Church leaders, including an agenda/issues page and four talking-point cards.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Utah Bush-Quayle Leadership Meeting talking points
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Two copies of five-card talking points prepare President Bush for the Utah Bush-Quayle Leadership Meeting.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Service Clubs Picnic speech draft for Jackson Hole
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A twenty-two-page Service Clubs Picnic speech draft prepares President Bush for remarks at Jackson Hole, Wyoming, on Friday, July 17, 1992.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush handwritten note to Charles L. Bartlett re Bill Moyers tape
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush tells Charles Bartlett that he watched the Moyers tape, did not think Clinton squirmed much, and sent the tape for campaign analysis.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush letter to A. W. Clausen re Advisory Committee for Trade Policy and Negotiations
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush thanks A. W. Clausen for service on the Advisory Committee for Trade Policy and Negotiations and for sharing his expertise with the committee.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Camp David presidential phone-call notes, July 19, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Three Camp David presidential phone-call sheets preserve Bush handwriting on campaign, Perot, Clinton, Russia, Philadelphia, and related follow-up points.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Pat Boone correspondence packet re Quayle, Dole, Powell, and Elizabeth Dole
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush answers Pat Boone's private suggestion that Dan Quayle step aside, with Boone's fax to Patty Presock and the President, a three-page argument for changing the ticket, and a July 20 follow-up naming Colin Powell and Elizabeth Dole.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Thomas S. Kleppe correspondence packet re Jim Baker and campaign management
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush replies to Thomas S. Kleppe's personal letter urging him to put Jim Baker in charge of the campaign, saying Baker is abroad after two fishing days in Wyoming and that the ball game is just beginning.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: C. Gregg Petersmeyer memo to President Bush re Points of Light Foundation commercials
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: C. Gregg Petersmeyer forwards new Points of Light Foundation television commercials and an advertisement list covering 1992 and 1991 spots.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush handwritten note to Bob Teeter re analyzing Clinton's charges
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush asks Bob Teeter to have someone analyze material giving him Clinton's charges against the President and the truth.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Campaign phone notes re vice-presidential speculation and Walter Annenberg
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A Camp David phone note and a typed message from Ambassador Walter Annenberg convey campaign and vice-presidential speculation, including Annenberg's dream that Dan Quayle stepped aside for Secretary Baker.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Camp David presidential phone and follow-up notes re Jack Steel and campaign issues
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The handwritten follow-up packet includes Camp David phone sheets, a typed Rose Zamaria update on Jack Steel's condition, and additional Bush handwriting on campaign, economic, education, and Tony Moody matters.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Thomas W. Moseley handwritten letter to President Bush
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Thomas W. Moseley of Gates Mills, Ohio, sends President Bush a two-page handwritten letter during the July campaign period.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Sara Bertagnolli correspondence and drawing packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush writes to Sara Bertagnolli of Lander, Wyoming, thanking her for a nice card and friendly note, with her hand-addressed drawing page attached.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Alan K. Simpson correspondence packet re Tony Principi recommendation
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush replies to Senator Alan Simpson about seeing him in Jackson and about Tony Principi, with Simpson's July 6 letter to the President, his recommendation letter to Constance Horner, and Rose Zamaria routing material attached.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Kristin Clark Taylor BellSouth correspondence
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Kristin Clark Taylor of BellSouth writes President Bush, with an address page and incoming BellSouth correspondence page in the packet.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Loret M. Ruppe wedding correspondence packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush writes Ambassador Loret M. Ruppe from Camp David thanking her for a note about Doro's wedding, with Ruppe's incoming note attached.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Charles L. Bartlett incoming letter and duplicate Bush note re Bill Moyers tape
directScanItemizedRecords.type: Correspondence Packet with Duplicate Presidential Note
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A second Charles L. Bartlett packet includes a duplicate of President Bush's Moyers tape note and Bartlett's incoming handwritten letter saying the tape looked good to him.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Buck correspondence packet re beer left on the kitchen table
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush writes Buck after returning from the West to thank him for beer left on the kitchen table, with Buck's handwritten note attached.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush note to Sam Skinner re Digger and Black Mayors meeting
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush asks Sam Skinner to have Digger invited to the Black Mayors meeting because of his work in cities on Weed and Seed.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush note to Brent Scowcroft re Grenada criminals
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush asks Brent Scowcroft where the trials or sentencing of Grenada criminals stand.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush notes to speechwriters re House control and Perot
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush tells the speechwriters to add more on Republicans not controlling the House for decades and to be careful about how much to keep talking about Perot.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Dan McGroarty memo and remarks draft for satellite broadcast to Bush-Quayle headquarters
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Dan McGroarty sends President Bush a memorandum and six-page draft remarks for the July 20 satellite broadcast to Bush-Quayle '92 campaign headquarters.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Telephone memoranda for President Bush, July 19, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: White House Signal Switchboard and President Bush telephone memorandum pages record July 19 calls involving David Bates, Barbara Bush, Neil Bush, Robert Mosbacher, Robert Teeter, and other entries.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential movements log: Camp David to Washington, July 19, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The movements log records President Bush's July 19 departure from Camp David by Marine One and arrival on the South Grounds in Washington.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: John Cardinal O'Connor letter copy re Knights of Columbus meeting and Pope John Paul II
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A Camp David-routed copy of John Cardinal O'Connor's July 16 letter looks forward to meeting President Bush at the Knights of Columbus Convention and conveys Pope John Paul II's gratitude for the President's prayerful concern.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Camp David phone-call note re Cardinal O'Connor
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A Camp David presidential phone-call sheet records handwritten follow-up connected to Cardinal O'Connor.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Duplicate President Bush note to Brent Scowcroft re Grenada criminals
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A duplicate or alternate copy repeats President Bush's question to Brent Scowcroft about the status of trials or sentencing for Grenada criminals.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Washington Post article packet: Mothers Struggle to Raise Sons Alone
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Washington Post clipping packet covers mothers raising sons alone, male role models, sons' defiance, and the limits of mothering without a male presence.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: USA Today Life cover story on adult literacy
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The USA Today Life cover story examines adult illiteracy, including the Home program segment and continuation material on people learning to fake literacy.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Newsday article: Homelessness, AIDS Linked
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A Newsday Associated Press article reports a National Commission on AIDS warning that many Americans with AIDS are homeless or close to homelessness.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: USA Today article: AIDS shows new signs that it can adapt
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A USA Today article from Amsterdam reports on new questions about the AIDS virus, including undetectable HIV cases and findings about heterosexual transmission.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush handwritten To Whom It May Concern campaign statement draft
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A sticky-note marker introduces a two-page handwritten campaign statement draft in which President Bush writes about a strenuous reelection campaign, integrity, honor, excitement, and pride in America.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House time capsule materials for the cornerstone anniversary
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The time-capsule packet includes Bush-signed images and family material, a From the desk of George Bush attachment marker, Anne Porter Rose's memo to Rose Zamaria, Mrs. Bush time-capsule notes, and a June 12 item list for the October 13 cornerstone anniversary.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: John Cardinal O'Connor correspondence and President Bush response packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes Camp David routing, Cardinal O'Connor's July 16 letter about the Knights of Columbus Convention and Pope John Paul II, a presidential response draft, an attachment marker, the White House envelope page, and a correspondence tracking worksheet.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Duplicate President Bush letter to Thomas S. Kleppe re Jim Baker
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A duplicate or alternate copy of the President's July 19 letter to Thomas S. Kleppe about Jim Baker appears with the White House address block.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Lawrence S. Eagleburger memorandum to President Bush re Poland, Azerbaijan, and Zaire
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Acting Secretary Lawrence S. Eagleburger briefs President Bush on Poland's small constitution, Azerbaijan's political situation, and Mobutu's resistance in Zaire.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Talking Points: USA Today interview
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The talking points frame the USA Today campaign discussion around trust, education, housing, economic growth, family values, health care, and peace.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Unpublished schedule of the President: Tuesday, August 4, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Phillip D. Brady forwards President Bush's unpublished August 4 schedule, covering briefings, veterans, USA Today, Bush/Quayle leaders, Freedom Support Act, campaign officials, and administrative time.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Pre-brief for meeting with veteran service organizations
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Leigh Ann Metzger prepares President Bush for issues that might arise before his Roosevelt Room roundtable with veterans service organization leaders.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Meeting with leaders of veterans service organizations briefing
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The briefing prepares President Bush to meet veterans service organization leaders, announce a White House working group on veterans health care, and discuss veterans issues.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Meeting with the Senate Republican Conference briefing
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Nicholas E. Calio prepares President Bush for an August 4 meeting with the Senate Republican Conference on the legislative agenda, economic growth package, health care initiatives, and campaign.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Pre-brief for interview with USA Today
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Marlin Fitzwater prepares President Bush for possible questions before his interview with USA Today's editorial board members and reporters.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Interview with USA Today briefing
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Marlin Fitzwater briefs President Bush for the Map Room USA Today interview, including participants, press plan, sequence, and the option of an opening statement.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Meeting with Bush/Quayle '92 Campaign Leadership Council briefing
directScanItemizedRecords.type: Campaign Meeting Briefing and Talking Points
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The briefing packet prepares President Bush for the Bush/Quayle '92 Leadership Council meeting and includes a business leader participant list and leadership council remarks.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Bipartisan congressional meeting on the Freedom Support Act briefing
directScanItemizedRecords.type: Congressional Meeting Briefing and Talking Points
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Nicholas E. Calio briefs President Bush for a bipartisan House meeting on passing the Freedom Support Act before the August recess, with participant list and talking points.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Weekly campaign meeting briefing
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Sam Skinner prepares President Bush for the August 4 weekly campaign meeting with the Vice President, campaign officials, and senior White House staff.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Phone message re Chase Untermeyer, Tiger Yang, and Yang Jiechi
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A White House phone message records Chase Untermeyer's note about Tiger Yang and Yang Jiechi, with President Bush handwriting directing follow-up.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential call notes for August 4, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The call note lists messages for President Bush from Senator Slade Gorton, Nicholas Brady, Governor John Sununu, and James Baker.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Materials Forwarded to the President: August 4, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The forwarded-materials list identifies action items, classified information, remarks, and schedule material sent to President Bush on August 4.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush handwritten anniversary note to Michael and Baina
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush sends a handwritten anniversary note to Michael and Baina, mentioning a 50th anniversary and a West Virginia getaway.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential Determination No. 92-37 re Haitian refugee applicants
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The press release transmits Presidential Determination No. 92-37 designating Haitian refugee applicants as qualifying for assistance under the Migration and Refugee Assistance Act.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press Release: President signs S. 2938 re U.S. Capitol Grounds
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces President Bush's signing of S. 2938, authorizing the Architect of the Capitol to acquire two lots for the U.S. Capitol Grounds.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press Release: President signs S. 992 re Jane E. Denne expenses
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces President Bush's signing of S. 992, providing reimbursement for Jane E. Denne's travel and relocation expenses.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press Release: President signs S.J. Res. 295 re National D.A.R.E. Day
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces President Bush's signing of S.J. Res. 295, designating September 10, 1992, as National D.A.R.E. Day.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Nomination release: Charles A. Felton for U.S. Marshal, Middle District of Florida
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces President Bush's intent to nominate Charles A. Felton to be U.S. Marshal for the Middle District of Florida.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Nomination release: Robert E. Wallace for Assistant Secretary of Labor
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces President Bush's intent to nominate Robert E. Wallace as Assistant Secretary of Labor for Veterans' Employment and Training.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Nomination release: J. Keith Gary for U.S. Marshal, Eastern District of Texas
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces President Bush's intent to renominate J. Keith Gary as U.S. Marshal for the Eastern District of Texas.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Nomination release: Edward S. Walker for deputy U.N. representative
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces President Bush's intent to nominate Edward S. Walker to be Deputy Representative of the United States to the United Nations with ambassadorial rank.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Statement by the President re child care rules
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush states that new rules will let parents receiving Child Care and Development Block Grant funds choose the child care they believe best for their children.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Nomination release: Roland Karl Kuchel for Ambassador to Haiti
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces President Bush's intent to nominate Roland Karl Kuchel as U.S. Ambassador to Haiti.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press Release: President signs H.R. 479 re Pony Express and California trails
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces President Bush's signing of H.R. 479, designating the Pony Express and California National Historic Trails as components of the National Trails System.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press briefing by Gail Wilensky and Kevin Moley re President Bush's health care plan
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The transcript records Gail Wilensky and Kevin Moley defending President Bush's health care plan, criticizing Clinton's plan, and answering questions about coverage, tax credits, small business, and Medicare and Medicaid funding.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Fred and Martha Zeder 50th wedding anniversary correspondence packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes President Bush's note to Fred and Martha Zeder, an anniversary invitation, a requested photograph page, a document-original marker, and related George Bush handwriting and routing notes.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Nicholas E. Calio memo and Alfonse D'Amato letter for President Bush's reading file
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Nicholas E. Calio forwards Senator Alfonse D'Amato's letter expressing regret over remarks that were interpreted as casting doubt on President Bush's leadership.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Bob Teeter memo forwarding Bobby Burchfield material re Americans for Change
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Bob Teeter forwards President Bush material from Bobby Burchfield about Americans for Change, with President Bush and staff handwritten annotations.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Mrs. Bush Daily Press Clippings: Tuesday, August 4, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Mrs. Bush press office packet collects August 4 clippings on Barbara Bush, generational politics, Hillary Clinton, First Lady coverage, AIDS, family policy, health care rationing, Tipper Gore, and related campaign themes.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Official White House photograph of President Bush in discussion, August 3, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A color Official White House photograph shows President Bush seated in discussion, with the stamped photo back page attached.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Official White House photograph of President Bush in the Oval Office, August 4, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A color Official White House photograph shows President Bush seated with two men in the Oval Office, with the stamped photo back page attached.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush note and Roger B. Porter follow-up re Michael Antonovich illegal aliens figures
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush asks Sam Skinner to respond to Michael Antonovich's fax about illegal aliens in Los Angeles County, and Roger B. Porter later reports on his telephone call with Antonovich.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Pool Report #2: Bush Goes Truckin'
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The pool report describes President Bush's Reno-area truck encounter and campaign-travel color during the August 5 trip.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press Release: Charles A. Gillespie Jr. appointed Special Assistant for National Security Affairs
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces Charles A. Gillespie Jr.'s appointment as Special Assistant to the President for National Security Affairs and Senior Director for Latin America and Caribbean Affairs at the NSC.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Nomination release: Robert W. Kostelka for U.S. District Judge
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces President Bush's intent to nominate Robert W. Kostelka as U.S. District Judge for the Western District of Louisiana.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press Release: President signs S. 249 re Trevor Henderson reimbursement
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces President Bush's signing of S. 249, reimbursing Trevor Henderson for a six-and-one-half-year period during which he was detailed overseas.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Point of Light release: Saint Vincent's Center of Timonium, Maryland
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House recognizes Saint Vincent's Center of Timonium, Maryland, as the 852nd Daily Point of Light for the Nation.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Remarks by President Bush to the Disabled American Veterans Convention
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The transcript records President Bush's remarks to the Disabled American Veterans Convention at the Reno Hilton Hotel in Reno, Nevada.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Remarks by President Bush to the Knights of Columbus Annual Supreme Council Convention
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The transcript records President Bush's remarks to the Knights of Columbus Annual Supreme Council Convention at the Marriott Marquis Hotel in New York.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Executive Order establishing the President's Commission on Management of AID Programs
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The executive order establishes the President's Commission on Management of the Agency for International Development (AID) Programs.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: William E. Edwards Tailhook correspondence packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes President Bush's reply to Commander William E. Edwards and Edwards's letter and Tailhook Association material about Tailhook investigations and naval service.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Archbishop Theodore E. McCarrick letter and report re former Yugoslavia
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Archbishop Theodore E. McCarrick writes President Bush after a mission to the former Yugoslavia and encloses a report on refugees, politics, and human rights conditions.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush handwritten note to John A. Gavin re Bohemian Grove
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush sends a handwritten note to John A. Gavin about a note from the Grove and future contact.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Jack Kramer Bohemian Grove note to President Bush
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Jack Kramer writes President Bush on Bohemian Grove stationery before the Houston convention.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Thomas F. Stroock Houston convention fax packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes President Bush notes to Thomas F. Stroock, White House and Embassy fax cover sheets, and Stroock letters about seeing the President at the Houston convention.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Daily News Clips: Wednesday, August 5, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The daily news clips packet collects August 5 coverage and commentary from the Los Angeles Times, USA Today, Washington Post, Wall Street Journal, New York Times, Washington Times, and related sources.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Andrew Charles Untener III correspondence, gift, and official photo packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush replies to Andrew Charles Untener III about campaign support in Georgia, a bronze artwork gift, Barbara Bush, and the Houston convention; the packet also includes attached official photo pages.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Pat Robertson CBN interview correspondence packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush thanks Pat Robertson for the CBN interview opportunity and responds to Robertson's July 28 letter reporting uniformly positive audience reaction.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Henry O. Dormann correspondence re National Enquirer articles and environmental conference proposal
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush thanks Henry O. Dormann for work with Bob Teeter on National Enquirer articles and routes Dormann's environmental conference proposal to Mike Deland.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Ted Stevens letter and President Bush reply re Wally Hickel endorsement
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush replies to Senator Ted Stevens about Wally Hickel's possible endorsement and the Houston convention.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Fife Symington supportive letter packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush thanks Arizona Governor Fife Symington for his supportive August 5 letter about media criticism, Clinton, the convention, and the campaign.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Thomas A. Sheridan environmental record correspondence packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush thanks Thomas A. Sheridan for his July 7 letter and says Shirley Green's material should help explain the administration's environmental record.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Bruce S. Gelb supportive letter packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush thanks Bruce S. Gelb for a supportive letter about the campaign, Clinton, press criticism, and Bush's qualifications for another term.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Mrs. Bush's Press Office Press Clippings: August 8-10, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Mrs. Bush press office packet collects August 8-10 clippings on Barbara Bush's Florida campaign travel, Bush-Clinton campaign coverage, Hillary Clinton, Anita Hill, child welfare, abortion, Mary Fisher and AIDS, and related convention themes.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Fred Latsko fax re Sid Luckman campaign involvement
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Fred Latsko writes President Bush about arranging for Sid Luckman to become involved in the campaign, with President Bush handwriting asking Ron to close the loop.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Michael R. Deland follow-up re Henry O. Dormann environmental conference proposal
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Michael R. Deland replies to Henry O. Dormann about the impracticality of a pre-election global business leaders conference and offers alternative environmental-policy channels.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Chi Chi Rodriguez endorsement follow-up packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush asks James A. Baker III to have the campaign follow up on Chi Chi Rodriguez's offer to endorse him, with a later Mimi Dawson memo reporting progress.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush letter to David C. Grimes re Bob Hope media suggestion
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush thanks David C. Grimes for warm words and says Bob Hope might be willing to help with a media suggestion that Bush has passed along.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: President Bush letter to Roger Ailes re John LeBoutillier and POW/MIA protesters
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: President Bush warns Roger Ailes about John LeBoutillier, Ron Kaufman, and recent POW/MIA demonstrations.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Official White House photographs from Kennebunkport, August 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet contains three color Official White House photographs from Kennebunkport, including a Madeleine Restaurant image, media/crowd scene, and outdoor speaking image, with corresponding photo backs.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Treasury forwarding note from Nicholas F. Brady re Houston Club remarks and Wall Street Journal articles
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Nicholas F. Brady forwards two Wall Street Journal articles and the Houston Club speech material, noting that the articles collect points the administration had been making.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Wall Street Journal articles on small business jobs and debt reduction
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Wall Street Journal packet includes 'For New Jobs, Help Small Business' and a companion article on the end of the American borrowing binge and debt reduction.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Nicholas F. Brady remarks to the Houston Club
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Treasury News text prepared for delivery records Nicholas F. Brady's Houston Club remarks on the economy, transition, savings and loan cleanup, debt, trade, the deficit, and values in the 1992 campaign.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press Release: President signs H.R. 4026 and H.R. 5566
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House press release announces the President's signing of H.R. 4026, the Zuni River Watershed Act, and H.R. 5566, concerning Catawba Indian Tribe land claims.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential Determination No. 92-38 re Zambia defense articles and services
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Presidential Determination No. 92-38 finds Zambia eligible to be furnished defense articles and services under the Foreign Assistance Act and Arms Export Control Act.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Message to Congress transmitting HHS report on the Radiation Control for Health and Safety Act
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The message transmits the Department of Health and Human Services report pursuant to section 540 of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act regarding radiation control.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Materials Forwarded to the President: August 11, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The forwarded-materials list identifies action items, classified information, and information items, including the Zuni and Catawba bills, a Derwinski certificate, a Jonathan Moore resignation acceptance, a Bolten Federal Implementation Plan memo, a Chernomyrdin item, campaign press material, procurement rules, the President's block schedule, and July producer-price numbers.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Points for calls to President Salinas and Prime Minister Mulroney re NAFTA agreement
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The call-points packet reports that negotiators had reached agreement on NAFTA and frames the agreement as a historic step toward a hemisphere-wide free trade area.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Signed Olympic team photograph and Rose Zamaria routing page
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes a signed color photograph of an Olympic team group, its annotated reverse, a handwritten note page, and a Rose Zamaria routing page.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Mrs. Bush's Press Office Daily Press Clippings: Tuesday, August 11, 1992

directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Constance Horner briefs the President on John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts Board of Trustees appointments, with membership lists and recommended nominees including Philip Anschutz, Stuart Bernstein, Alma Powell, Janie R. Ikard, and Barbara Barrett.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary: 6:00 a.m. EDT edition, September 14, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The morning News Summary covers trip news, Reagan and Dornan at Yorba Linda, Bush and Clinton campaign attacks, endangered-species and timber coverage, Hurricane Iniki, health care, federal salaries, IRAs, Israel and Saudi arms sales, IMF deficit criticism, Bosnia, network news, and weekend television transcripts.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Congressional Monitor, September 14, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Congressional Monitor packet covers the prior week in Congress, upcoming primaries, appropriations, committee meetings, conference committee listings, campaign future listings, appropriations status, House and Senate floor action, and the September 16 weekly schedule.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary: 7:30 a.m. trip update, September 14, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A trip-oriented White House News Summary page from San Diego summarizes early campaign and trip coverage before the President's Pacific Northwest stops.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary: 2:30 p.m. PDT / 5:30 p.m. EDT news, September 14, 1992

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Fact sheet: Caring for America's Forests

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Remarks by the President at Colville, Washington, September 14, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Colville remarks address the spotted owl, timber jobs, the Endangered Species Act, agriculture, and a brief AIDS exchange during the President's Pacific Northwest campaign swing.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Proclamation: Commodore John Barry Day, September 13, 1992

directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Orange County rally remarks cover the economy, MIAs, taxes, missile defense, Clinton, and campaign themes for the California stop.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Office of the Press Secretary copies of Burrill Lumber Company remarks
