directScanItemizedRecords.title: Press Release: President signs H.R. 5237, H.R. 5739, and H.R. 3665
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House announces the President's signing of H.R. 5237 on Rural Electrification Administration loan prepayment, H.R. 5739 on Export-Import Bank and export promotion programs, and H.R. 3665 creating Little River Canyon National Preserve.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Memorandum of Disapproval for S. 3095, Jena Band of Choctaws of Louisiana Restoration Act
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President withholds approval of S. 3095, arguing that recognition of the Jena Band of Choctaws should proceed through the established Federal acknowledgement process.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Executive Order: Transfer of Certain Iraqi Government Assets Held by Domestic Banks
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The executive order directs actions to transfer certain blocked Iraqi government funds and assets held by domestic banks under U.N. Security Council Resolution 778.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Alan Reynolds column: The Worst Lies About the Economy in the Past 50 Years
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A clipped Alan Reynolds column argues that Bill Clinton's debate claims about the economy are inaccurate.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Schedule of the President: Wednesday, October 21, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The schedule lists the President's October 21 whistlestop and North Carolina events, including Spartanburg, Gastonia, Kannapolis, Thomasville, Burlington, and Raleigh.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Recommended telephone call to Electronic Industries Association Board of Governors
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The recommended-call packet asks the President to call the Electronic Industries Association Board of Governors to thank the industry for support and discuss exports, R&D, tax policy, high-tech employment, and endorsements.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: James A. Baker III memorandum and draft remarks: The Choice on November Third
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: James A. Baker III sends the President a draft speech titled 'The Choice on November Third: Philosophy, Policy, Character,' arguing for a final contrast with Clinton.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Handwritten note from Margaret Tutwiler to the President
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Margaret Tutwiler thanks the President for kind remarks and encouragement in a handwritten note.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Pool reports: Gastonia, State Fair, and Raleigh, October 21, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Pool reports cover the President at a Gastonia Waffle House, the North Carolina State Fair, and the Raleigh motorcade to the Radisson.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Mrs. Bush's Press Office Daily Press Clippings: Wednesday, October 21, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Mrs. Bush's Press Office clipping packet includes Republican events, ice-cream polling, Clinton flag-burning charges and apologies, Prescott Bush, Hillary Clinton, women in politics, and campaign-family stories.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Alec P. Courtelis correspondence packet re final debate and Clinton character issues
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The packet includes the President's response to Alec Courtelis, Courtelis's October 19 personal fax, and advice on character arguments after the last debate.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Lauren correspondence re campaign train and school campaign report
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President writes Lauren about being on the train with her father, a television report, and Lauren's report on the campaign in school, with an incoming handwritten page attached.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Mortgage discrimination talking points
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Talking points marked as seen by the President address mortgage discrimination, OCC advisories, fair-lending enforcement, and Administration actions.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Loose campaign press clippings on debates, Iraq, tax, Clinton, Perot, defense, and the campaign trail
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A loose clipping run draws from The Washington Post, The New York Times, Los Angeles Times, The Washington Times, Newsday, and other outlets on the presidential debates, voter anger, tax legislation, Iraq/Kuwait cables, Clinton and Perot, campaign strategy, and military and media issues.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: U.S. News and World Report campaign and Iraqgate article packet
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: U.S. News and World Report pages include best-jobs cover material, debate spin, Washington Whispers, Clinton campaign analysis, Iraqgate reporting, and Mortimer Zuckerman's Arrogance of Power editorial.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Wall Street Journal articles on Clinton, markets, GATT, the Bush campaign, and the economy
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Wall Street Journal packet covers Clinton's compensation-tax position, bond-market expectations, the presidential homestretch, GATT, Bush campaign missteps, and Alan Reynolds on the economy.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Loose education, media, and broadcasting clippings
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Loose clippings include USA Today education-policy arguments, taxes and deficit commentary, Time material, Broadcasting articles on television and FCC issues, and media-business pages.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: New Republic articles on James Baker, Clinton, and conservatism
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The New Republic packet includes Sidney Blumenthal on James Baker, Joshua Muravchik on supporting Bill Clinton, and articles on conservatism and the First Amendment.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Official White House photo transfer pages, October 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Official White House photo pages and transfer placeholders appear at the end of the direct folder scan, including campaign-event photographs with handwritten location notes.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Acceptances for Manor House Reception
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: An acceptance list for the Manor House reception, updated as of 12:55 p.m. on election day, records family, staff, campaign, and supporter attendees.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Fax from Debbie Dunn to Bobbie Kilberg with Bush-Quayle family and friends contact list
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Bush Quayle '92 fax cover sheet from Debbie Dunn to Bobbie Kilberg, followed by a family and friends contact list for the November 3 file.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: LeRoy B. Shaw correspondence re Chad photo and campaign support
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President sends LeRoy B. Shaw a signed photo for Chad, wishes Chad well at Duke, and thanks the Shaws for encouragement and support.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Memorandum from Rose Zamaria to the President re Bill Moss election-night party
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Rose Zamaria reports that Bill Moss wanted to underwrite an election-night party, but appeared to lose interest when told the President would be there for only 20 or 30 minutes.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential Phone Calls handwritten note
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A Presidential Phone Calls form appears with George Bush handwriting and brief subject/follow-up fields.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Memorandum from Rose Zamaria to Jim Baker re Bill Moss election-night party
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Rose Zamaria asks Jim Baker whether Bill Moss can go forward with an election-night party for family, old friends, and staff at the Houstonian.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Concession Address draft with George Bush handwriting
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A draft of the Houston concession address includes the opening call to Governor Clinton, thanks to campaign and administration leaders, and extensive George Bush handwriting.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Handwritten note from the President to Rose Zamaria re getting back to Moss
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President asks Rose Zamaria to remind him to get back to Moss in a week or so.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Hugh Liedtke correspondence re Betty Liedtke memorial service
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A President Has Seen incoming letter from Hugh Liedtke thanks George Bush for attending Betty Liedtke's memorial service, with related letterhead and envelope pages.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to William P. Moss re election night at the Houstonian
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President thanks William Moss for offering to give an election-night party at the Houstonian and says he will let Moss know very soon what the plans are.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Memorandum from Rose Zamaria to Secret Service re Peter and Edith O'Donnell
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Rose Zamaria alerts the Secret Service that Peter and Edith O'Donnell are expected that evening and asks that they not be detained.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from William P. Moss to the President re election-night party
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: William P. Moss proposes an election-night party for the President and Barbara Bush with old friends, family, and past and present staff.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary: November 3, 1992 1:30 P.M. CST News Update
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The 1:30 p.m. CST White House News Summary update covers President Bush in Houston, Clinton voting, the abortion gag-rule decision, women in combat, Iraq/BNL, and Clinton Middle East comments.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Memorandum from Bobbie Kilberg to Rose Zamaria re update on the '80 Bush Brigade project
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Bobbie Kilberg describes the project to locate 1980 Bush supporters, enlist their help in getting out the vote, and invite the '80 Bush Brigade to Houston for election night.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary: November 3, 1992 10:00 A.M. CST News Update
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The 10:00 a.m. CST White House News Summary update covers election-day voting in Houston, the CBS poll, economic indicators, Vice President Quayle, and the Villalpando inquiry.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Memorandum from Karen Groomes to David Bates, Kathy Super, and John Keller re Bobbie Kilberg memo
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Karen Groomes forwards Bobbie Kilberg's memo for action now that President Bush would be in Houston on election eve.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: White House News Summary: Tuesday, November 3, 1992 6:00 A.M. EST Edition
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The 6:00 a.m. EST White House News Summary compiles election-day trip, national, international, and network news coverage on Bush, Clinton, Perot, voter turnout, and related issues.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Memorandum from Bobbie Kilberg to Margaret Tutwiler re early supporters of President Bush
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Bobbie Kilberg recommends identifying early supporters, contacting them about the campaign, and inviting them to Houston for election night.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Talking points re negative advertising and Clinton campaign charges
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Election-day talking points argue that Bush-Quayle advertisements are documented and accurate while Clinton campaign advertisements are false.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Mrs. Bush's Press Office Daily Press Clippings: Tuesday, November 3, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Mrs. Bush's Press Office daily press clippings for Tuesday, November 3, 1992, including election and First Family coverage.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Fact Paper: The Weinberger Additional Indictment
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A fact paper argues that the Weinberger note adds no new facts to the Iran-Contra record and is filed with Congressional Report excerpts and a chronology of Vice President Bush's exposure to the Shultz-Weinberger position.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from the President to Thomas J. Devine re Devine's October 28 letter
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The President tells Thomas J. Devine that his October 28 letter caught up with him in Houston and that staff would look into it and give him a report.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Washington Post article clipping re the President's side of Iran-Contra
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A Washington Post clipping argues that Bush did not know of the Iran-Contra diversion until just before it was revealed and distinguishes the Iran initiative from the diversion.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from Thomas J. Devine to the President re Soviet nuclear-powered submarines
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Thomas J. Devine urges presidential attention to nuclear weapons and reactors in lost Soviet submarines and the Woods Hole Oceanographic Institution's proposed conference.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter from C. Boyden Gray to the Washington Post editor re Iran-Contra reporting
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: C. Boyden Gray writes that the Post mischaracterized President Bush's comments to Katie Couric about Iran-Contra and the January 1986 meeting.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Media Affairs Office News Clippings: Election Day, Tuesday, November 3, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Media Affairs Office election-day news clippings for Tuesday, November 3, 1992, beginning with a presidential candidate comparison and national newspaper coverage.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Key Facts on Iraqgate with supporting CCC and Clinton quotables material
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A Key Facts on Iraqgate packet argues there was no cover-up of pre-war Iraq policy and includes supporting material on CCC guarantees and Clinton quotations.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Pool Report #27: Air Force One flight from Akron to Louisville
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Mike Kranish reports on the uneventful Air Force One flight from Akron to Louisville and the motorcade to the rally hangar.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Pool Report #29: The President jogs, votes, and shops
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Pool Report #29 describes President Bush jogging in Memorial Park, voting at St. Mary's Seminary, breakfasting at the Houstonian, shopping, and the planned Manor House reception.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Pool Report #30: Bush in corridor of Houstonian Hotel
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Pool Report #30 records President Bush in a Houstonian Hotel corridor at about 6:00 p.m. and his brief comment on how he felt.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: The Hotline Daily Briefing on American Politics: Election Day Reader
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The Hotline election-day briefing compiles final presidential polls, an electoral scoreboard, campaign-final-lap coverage, Senate and House race notes, governors' races, and poll updates.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential telephone memorandum packet for November 3, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A telephone memorandum packet records election-day calls involving Nicholas Brady, Henry Kissinger, Dan Quayle, James Baker, Marlin Fitzwater, Robert Dole, Brian Mulroney, Bill Clinton, and others.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Presidential Movements: Houston, Texas, November 3, 1992
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The presidential movements sheet records President Bush's Houstonian Hotel, St. Mary's Seminary, shopping, and Westin Galleria movements on election day.

directScanItemizedRecords.title: Bush-Quayle '92 campaign materials catalog and order form
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: A Bush-Quayle '92 campaign materials catalog and order form lists lapel stickers, campaign buttons, bumper stickers, yard signs, apparel, and related merchandise.

directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: Dear David: I am pleased to write a letter of recommendation inasmuch as I have known you well for many years and worked with you on numerous projects. I can strongly vouch for your integrity, high moral standards, hard working habits and good judgement. You have an excellent personality and the ability to work effectively with your associates. I have been impressed with your organizational competence in the world of business. I will be glad to respond to anyone wishing a more detailed endorsement. Warmest, best...

directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: The White House 1600 Pennsylvania Avenue Washington, D.C. 20005 Dear Mr. President, I am writing to ask you for assistance to aid me in finding employment. directScanItemizedRecords.title: Letter: Dear Mr. President
directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: THE WHITE HOUSE WASHINGTON Dear Mr. President: Thank you for your kind invitation to the December 15 Reconciliation Ceremony in San Salvador. While I will be unable to attend the ceremony, I am pleased to have Vice President Dan Quayle represent me. You have worked long and hard to obtain a just and lasting peace for El Salvador, and I share the joy of all Salvadoran citizens that your goal is now clearly within reach. directScanItemizedRecords.excerpt: News Summary OFFICE OF THE PRESS SECRETARY THE WHITE HOUSE WASHINGTON THURSDAY, JANUARY 14, 1993 6:00 A.M. EST EDITION INTERNATIONAL NEWS U.S. DELIVERS LIMITED AIR STRIKE ON IRAQ -- After a week of brinkmanship in the waning days of his term, President Bush Wednesday dispatched Air Force and Navy warplanes to bomb air defense targets in southern Iraq, dealing a limited blow to the regime of Saddam Hussein.
